title: Share browser move command construction between probes and confirmation
description: src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc builds partial commands for resolver-backed target and option checks around lines 677, 844, 971, and 1459, then builds final confirmation commands again around lines 6050-6305. Moon and World paths add more bespoke reconstruction. The duplicated command knowledge can drift, so a target can look legal in staging while confirmation sends a subtly different command. Acceptance: introduce canonical command fragments or a single builder with explicit partial/final modes, migrate option probes and confirmation to share it, and add tests that prove resolver probes and final commands stay aligned for Rod, Disc, Sword, Sun, Moon, and World cases.
close_reason: Shared move-selection resolver probes and final confirmation through canonical command builders, with Rod/Disc/Sword/Sun/Moon/World regression coverage.

title: Allow one local user to control two players for dev testing
description: Add a development/testing-only option that lets a single local user drive two player seats in the browser. This should make it easier to exercise turn flow, move selection, and multi-player interactions without coordinating multiple humans. Keep the behavior clearly scoped to local/dev fixtures or lobby options so normal game rules and production multiplayer assumptions are not changed.
close_reason: Implemented dev shared local control lobby option

title: Epic: Implement Sword gameplay logic
description:
Track the remaining Sword/Swords gameplay work as separate implementation tickets. Current state: resolve-sword-command validates browser-free Sword commands in src/main/gnostica/game_state/sword.cljc, while browser move selection still returns :move-transition-unavailable for Sword powers.

Split work:
- Pure piece attack transition.
- Pure territory attack transition.
- Major Sword variant and sequencing edge cases.
- Browser Sword staging and confirmation wiring after pure contracts settle.
acceptance_criteria:
- Child tickets exist for the pure piece attack, pure territory attack, Sword major/variant edge cases, and browser staging work.
- The epic remains open until those tickets are implemented, tested, and the docs/mind map are reconciled.
close_reason: All child Sword implementation tickets are closed; current docs and code track remaining browser major work under bd-ydt.

title: Implement Sword piece attack transition
description:
Implement pure Sword attacks against piece targets on top of the existing resolve-sword-command validation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add or extend the public apply-sword-move facade so resolved :piece targets mutate pure game state through structured success/error maps.
- Shrink large pieces to medium and medium pieces to small; destroy small pieces.
- Return removed/replaced pieces to the correct owner stash and keep player stash mirrors plus [:pieces :stashes] reconciled.
- Preserve enemy survivor orientation; allow only resolver-validated current-player survivor orientation.
- Discard hand-source Sword cards exactly once and append useful Sword history events.
- Add focused unit and/or feature coverage for success paths, over-damage/rejection paths already normalized by the resolver, and Malli schema/stash accounting invariants.
close_reason: Implemented pure Sword piece attack transition

title: Implement Sword territory attack transition
description:
Implement pure Sword attacks against territory targets on top of the existing resolve-sword-command validation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extend apply-sword-move so resolved :territory targets mutate pure game state through structured success/error maps.
- Reduce territory card value by selected damage for non-destroying attacks using a valid replacement card.
- Destroy territories when damage equals the territory value, moving the destroyed card to discard and preserving valid surrounding board/piece invariants.
- Require replacement cards for non-destroying attacks; reject missing, reused, or invalid replacements without mutating state.
- Support Tower discard-pile replacement rules while keeping normal Sword replacement sources limited to hand cards.
- Preserve legal pieces and orientations on surviving territory coordinates.
- Add focused unit and/or feature coverage for success paths, replacement rejection paths, destruction, and deck/discard/schema accounting.
close_reason: done

title: Cover Sword major variants and sequencing edge cases
description:
Close the rule gaps around major-arcana Sword variants after the base piece and territory attack transitions exist.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verify Justice, Death, and Moon plain Sword powers use the same attack contract where appropriate.
- Verify Tower uses the discard-pile replacement option only where resolve-sword-command allows it.
- Verify Magician wild-suit Sword behavior is carried through from gnostica.cards/sword-variants into attack application.
- Add focused tests for variant identity in history/events and for source-card discard behavior.
- Document any deliberately deferred multi-step or browser-staged major behavior in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if implementation is intentionally partial.
close_reason: done

title: Wire browser Sword staging and confirmation
description:
Implement browser move-selection support for Sword attacks after the pure apply-sword-move contract is available.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add staged Sword controls for target kind, target piece/territory, damage, replacement source/card, and current-player survivor orientation where applicable.
- Build app-state confirmation commands that call apply-sword-move and update :game only on success.
- Preserve structured gameplay errors on rejected confirmations without clearing the staged selection.
- Remove the Sword-specific :move-transition-unavailable path only for the implemented Sword confirmation flow.
- Keep Sun unavailable behavior unchanged.
- Add app-state/move-selection tests and browser smoke coverage where practical for the Sword panel flow.
close_reason: done

title: Clarify release build prerequisites for server and smoke docs
description: README.md and AGENTS.md list clojure -M:server and describe clojure -M:smoke as serving the released app, but they do not make the prerequisite explicit: a current browser build must already exist, normally from clojure -M:release, unless SMOKE_URL targets an existing dev or release server. Tighten the command docs so stale or missing src/main/resources/js/main.js is an obvious failure mode.
close_reason: Documented release build prerequisite for server and smoke commands

title: Move-panel re-frame subscription drops control groups
description: The pure app-state move-panel view includes :control-groups via app-state/move-panel-view, but the re-frame :gnostica.app/move-panel-view subscription in src/main/gnostica/app/events.cljs never subscribes to or passes control groups into app-state/move-panel-view-model. gnostica.ui.move-panel renders active controls by iterating (:control-groups view), so the browser path can show a selected source/prompt without the staged controls even though app-state tests pass. Acceptance: expose control groups through the re-frame subscription, keep the view-model shape identical between app-state and re-frame, and add browser/re-frame coverage that fails if :control-groups is missing.
close_reason: Fixed move-panel control groups subscription and added browser smoke coverage for staged hand-card controls

title: Restore clojure-lsp diagnostics as a clean verification signal
description: A fresh clojure-lsp diagnostics run exits nonzero even though clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke pass. Current diagnostics include a redundant boolean coercion in app_state.cljc, the unused Death sword-action-count UI var/binding in move_panel.cljs, and unused bindings in gesture_intent.cljc and move_selection.cljc. Some warnings are symptoms of real drift; the rest should be removed or explicitly configured so diagnostics are useful again. Acceptance: clojure-lsp diagnostics exits zero, or remaining intentional warnings are documented/configured and do not mask new regressions.
close_reason: Removed stale lsp diagnostics and verified clojure-lsp plus tests pass

title: Allow changing dragged piece orientation with keyboard
description:
When a player is dragging a piece, they should be able to change the pending piece orientation without leaving the drag flow.

Scope:
- Support arrow-key orientation changes while a piece drag is active: Up should choose upright/up, and the cardinal arrows should choose north/east/south/west where applicable.
- Add a separate hotkey that cycles through all five legal orientation states: up, north, east, south, west. Choose a key that does not conflict with existing board navigation or browser shortcuts.
- Apply this to first-piece initial placement drags and any direct-manipulation piece movement flow where the resulting command accepts current-player orientation.
- Keep enemy-piece movement behavior unchanged: enemy orientations must stay preserved when rules disallow reorientation.
- Make the active drag preview, destination/drop preview, pending tray, and final confirmation command all reflect the selected orientation.

Acceptance criteria:
- During a legal first-piece drag, pressing arrow keys changes the dragged pyramid orientation before drop.
- During applicable own-piece drags, orientation hotkeys update the staged orientation consistently with move-selection validation.
- The cycle hotkey rotates through all five legal states and wraps predictably.
- Keyboard orientation changes are ignored or visibly rejected when the active drag cannot legally choose orientation.
- Existing camera/board keyboard controls continue to work when no drag is active.
- Add focused app-state/gesture or browser smoke coverage for first-piece drag orientation selection and at least one non-initial own-piece drag path if practical.
close_reason: done

title: Split move-selection monolith into focused namespaces
description: src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc is 6,946 lines and owns source and power definitions, option derivation, requirement and stage progression, legal target descriptors, command construction, previews, and transition dispatch. Examples: control groups around lines 2134-2289, descriptors around 5479-6030, command builders around 6050-6333, and preview/dispatch around 6432-6945. This coupling makes rule changes difficult to review because UI staging, legality hints, and final transitions are interleaved. Acceptance: extract at least command building and dispatch, target descriptor derivation, and option/control-group derivation into focused namespaces or modules with stable public APIs. Keep the app-state facade usable by existing callers and keep clojure -M:test passing.
close_reason: Extracted move-selection controls, target descriptors, command building, and transition dispatch into focused namespaces while keeping facade APIs and tests passing.

title: Antialias 3D board rendering
description:
Goal: make the Three.js board renderer produce visibly antialiased card, wasteland, and Icehouse piece edges.

Requirements:
- Enable antialiasing in the WebGL renderer where supported without breaking the existing r128 Three.js global runtime.
- Preserve the CSS fallback path for unavailable or incompatible WebGL runtimes.
- Verify canvas sizing and nonblank rendering still pass on desktop and mobile.
- Keep browser-free board layout math unchanged unless antialiasing exposes a tested geometry issue.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D board canvas uses antialiasing where the browser/GPU supports it.
- Card and piece outlines look smoother without changing selection behavior or layout.
- clojure -M:release and clojure -M:smoke pass.
close_reason: Implemented antialiased renderer setup with smoke coverage

title: Move oversized test fixture builders into shared test support
description: test/gnostica/app_state_test.clj and test/gnostica/game_state_test.clj are each over 5,100 lines and duplicate fixture/building helpers such as deck-with-cards-at, piece-by-id, board-cell-by-index, and player hand mirror updates. app_state_test.clj also reads source forms around lines 138-158 to verify move-panel renderer coverage, which couples tests to private function names and file layout. Acceptance: create shared test support namespaces for deck, board, piece, and app-db fixtures; move duplicated helpers there; split the largest test files by feature or move family where practical; replace source-form parsing coverage with registry/data coverage if bd-zdu1 is completed first.
close_reason: Extracted duplicated deck, board, piece, game-state, and app-db test fixture helpers into shared test_support namespaces; replaced move-panel source parsing coverage with registry data coverage; clojure -M:test passes.

title: Split the Three.js board renderer out of gnostica.app
description:
Problem: `src/main/gnostica/app.cljs` mixes re-frame events/subscriptions, UI components, Three.js runtime detection, board layout constants, texture loading, mesh construction, pointer raycasting, camera controls, and disposal in one namespace. The renderer is working, but the current shape makes it harder to review lifecycle safety, test pure layout decisions, and extend gameplay UI without touching WebGL internals.

Evidence:
- `three-board-scene` and helpers occupy most of `app.cljs`, from Three runtime helpers through component lifecycle and fallback status UI.
- Pure layout rules such as card positions, piece slots, and selection mapping are embedded in browser/Three-specific code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move Three.js-specific scene creation, renderer lifecycle helpers, and board/piece layout constants into a dedicated renderer namespace.
- Keep `gnostica.app` focused on app state, subscriptions, and UI composition.
- Extract any pure layout/mapping helpers that can be tested without a browser or WebGL context.
- Add focused tests for extracted pure helpers where practical, especially card positioning, board index mapping, and piece slot limits.
- MIND_MAP.md and extern notes are updated if namespace ownership or Three.js API usage changes.
close_reason: Split renderer into gnostica.three-board, extracted browser-free board-layout helpers, added layout tests, and updated README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP docs.

title: No-piece turns can still expose draw when hand has room
description: The local rules say that if a player has no pieces on the board, they may only place a small piece on an empty territory or wasteland. The move-selection source availability only disables activate/play/orient when there are no owned pieces. Draw cards is enabled whenever max-draw-count is positive, so a no-piece player with fewer than six cards could draw instead of placing the initial small. This is masked at fresh setup because hands start full, but it becomes a real state-machine bug once piece destruction or other card movement creates a no-piece, under-full hand. Evidence: docs/rules.txt lines 47 to 55, README.md line 55, AGENTS.md line 39, and src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc source-unavailable-reason and max-draw-count. Enforce the no-piece restriction in source options and confirmation, then add an app-state test for a no-piece player with hand room.
close_reason: Fixed no-piece draw availability and confirmation guard

title: Draw faint dotted outlines around wasteland squares
description:
Problem: Wasteland spaces are rule-relevant empty squares adjacent to territories, but the board UI currently emphasizes only occupied territory cards. Players need a subtle visual cue for legal wasteland spaces without making the board feel cluttered.

Acceptance criteria:
- Identify board squares that are wastelands: empty spaces adjacent to at least one territory and not void spaces.
- Draw a faint dotted outline around each wasteland square in the primary Three.js board view.
- Add a matching faint dotted outline in the CSS fallback board view.
- Keep outlines behind cards and pieces so they do not interfere with selection, raycasts, piece placement, card readability, or future move-target highlighting.
- Make the outline visible enough on desktop and mobile but visually secondary to territory cards, selected cards, and pieces.
- Add focused browser-free layout/state tests if wasteland detection is extracted, and update smoke coverage or manual notes if renderer behavior changes.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if new board-layout helpers, renderer behavior, or verification guidance are added.
close_reason: Implemented browser-free wasteland detection, 3D dashed outlines, CSS fallback dotted outlines, docs, release build, and smoke verification.

title: Align README startup description with lobby-first browser flow
description:
Fresh-eye docs review found README has stale pre-lobby board-first wording. README.md:25 says the first screen renders the nine-card board, and README.md:29 says local browser startup opts into demo board pieces. Current app code and other docs say normal gnostica.fixtures/browser-init-options starts in a two-player lobby with no game/demo pieces; only smoke options bypass the lobby with :demo-board-pieces, and app-state/initialize applies demo pieces only when explicit options provide them. README.md:3 also still calls move selection early even though browser staging now covers nearly all implemented powers.

Acceptance: revise the README overview and 3D board section to say the normal first screen is the local lobby, the board appears after start or fixture bypass, demo pieces are explicit fixture/smoke data, and move-selection maturity wording matches AGENTS.md and MIND_MAP.md.
close_reason: Aligned README overview and 3D board docs with lobby-first startup, explicit fixture bypass, and current move-selection coverage.

title: Implement Cup move gameplay rules
description: Add pure gameplay support for Cup moves in gnostica.game-state and the shared confirmation path. Scope covers adding the current player's small piece to a territory and adding a one-point card to an eligible targeted wasteland that is not occupied by enemy minions. Acceptance: transitions return explicit success/failure maps, preserve schema, deck, and piece invariants, append history events, and keep rendering-specific work out of the gameplay layer.
close_reason: Cup move gameplay rules and app-state confirmation are implemented

title: Define Cup move command contract and tests
description: Introduce the browser-free command/action shape and coverage for both Cup move variants. Acceptance: source card or territory, target, orientation, and selected one-point card parameters are explicit, card helpers identify Cups and one-point cards, invalid commands return structured errors, and unit or feature tests cover successful small-piece placement, successful wasteland-card placement, and the key rejection cases.
close_reason: done

title: Implement Cup move small-piece placement
description: Implement the Cup effect that places the current player's small piece on a target territory. Acceptance: requires an available small piece in stash, requires an existing territory target, validates orientation, creates a deterministic unique piece id, keeps player and pieces stashes consistent, enforces the per-space piece limit, appends a history event, and leaves the resulting game schema-valid.
close_reason: done

title: Represent wasteland targets in gameplay state
description: Promote wasteland target addressing into pure gameplay helpers so game-state can resolve targeted wastelands without browser-only state. Acceptance: commands can identify a wasteland by stable row and column coordinates adjacent to the current board, piece occupancy can be checked for that space, board expansion can assign a stable new territory index, and board_layout rendering remains compatible with the expanded board.
close_reason: done

title: Implement Cup move one-point wasteland card placement
description: Implement the Cup effect that turns an eligible targeted wasteland into a territory with a one-point card. Acceptance: consumes the selected one-point card from the command-defined source, rejects targets occupied by enemy minions, permits friendly or empty occupancy according to the encoded rule, creates a face-up board cell with board-derived orientation, preserves unique card accounting, appends a history event, and tests success plus enemy-minion rejection.
close_reason: done

title: Wire Cup move results through app-state confirmation
description: Replace the Cup move happy path in app-state confirmation with the implemented game-state transition instead of :move-transition-unavailable. Acceptance: completed Cup commands update :game on success, rejected commands keep the staged selection with the structured gameplay error, card zones and board subscriptions continue deriving from :game, and tests cover both success and rejection without duplicating gameplay rules in app_state.
close_reason: Wired Cup move confirmation through app-state

title: Add start-game first-player bidding flow
description:
The local browser lobby currently starts the game directly and only reaches official first-player bidding when tests or callers inject :starting-bids into gnostica.game-state/create-game. docs/rules.txt says the full start flow should let every player choose a face-down bid card, reveal bids, rank major arcana above minors, repeat full-table bidding on tied top bids, redraw bid cards counterclockwise from the winner right, and rotate the winner into the first turn.

Implement a player-facing setup flow for deciding who goes first while keeping the existing pure game-state starting-bids contract stable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser setup exposes a clear optional start-game bidding step after lobby player and target-score selection, with a simple pick-first shortcut still available for first games or agreed casual play.
- Each local player can choose one bid card from their initial six-card hand without mutating :game until the start decision is confirmed.
- Revealed bids use the existing game-state major/minor ranking and tie-rebid behavior instead of duplicating rule logic in UI namespaces.
- Bid redraw order follows the existing game-state starting-bids implementation: bid cards are redrawn counterclockwise from the winner right, hands return to six cards, and the winner becomes current player.
- App-state and re-frame handlers keep deterministic shuffle/deck boundaries explicit and remain browser-free where practical.
- Add focused app-state/game-state/browser-staging tests and update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the setup workflow changes.
close_reason: Implemented lobby first-player bidding flow with tests and docs

title: Strength and Star Disc major behaviors are not stageable in the move panel
description: The pure Disc transition supports Strength via :disc-actions with one or two action maps and same-target shortcuts, and Star via :minion-orientation before Disc validation plus discard-pile replacement. The browser move-selection layer only builds a single disc-command, exposes target orientation for grown current-player pieces, and does not provide controls for a second Strength action or Star's minion-orientation step. Star discard-pile replacement is exposed, but the orient-before-targeting part is not. Evidence: README.md line 51, AGENTS.md line 31, MIND_MAP.md node [46], src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc disc-requirements, disc-command, move-disc-orientation-available?, and app-state tests that cover Star replacement but not minion orientation or Strength double-action UI. Either add browser staging for these major-specific Disc flows or mark them as pure-only/unavailable so the UI does not imply full major-card support.
close_reason: Implemented browser staging for Strength Disc actions and Star minion orientation.

title: Implement Rods gameplay rules
description: Add pure gameplay support for Rods moves in gnostica.game-state and the shared confirmation path. Scope covers moving the acting minion, pushing a target piece, and pushing a territory according to the minion direction and pip count from docs/rules.txt. Acceptance: transitions return explicit success/failure maps, preserve schema, deck, board, and piece/stash invariants, append history events, reject upright Rod minions, enforce destination capacity and void/wasteland rules, handle optional orientation only for the current player's moved pieces, and keep renderer-specific work out of the gameplay layer.
close_reason: done

title: Define Rods command contract and legality helpers
description: Introduce the browser-free command/action shape and reusable legality helpers for Rods. Acceptance: commands explicitly identify player id, Rod source as territory or hand card, acting minion piece id, Rod mode as move-minion, push-piece, or push-territory, target piece or territory coordinates where needed, positive distance within the minion pip count, and optional orientation for current-player moved pieces. Tests cover command normalization, Rod card detection, upright-minion rejection, zero-distance rejection, invalid direction/distance rejection, and structured errors without mutating game state.
close_reason: Implemented Rod command normalization and legality helper tests

title: Implement Rods piece movement and piece pushing
description: Implement Rods effects that move the acting minion or push a target piece in the minion's facing direction. Acceptance: distance is one through the acting minion's pip count, upright Rod minions cannot move or push, destinations must not be void and must not be territories with three or more pieces, wasteland destinations remain represented consistently, moving an owned piece may set a validated new orientation, pushing an enemy piece preserves its orientation, piece owner and space mirrors remain consistent, history events are appended, and resulting states remain schema-valid.
close_reason: Implemented Rods piece movement and piece pushing

title: Implement Rods territory pushing
description: Implement the Rods effect that pushes a target territory in the acting minion's facing direction. Acceptance: target territory must not be occupied by enemy pieces, distance is one through the acting minion's pip count, the territory may pass through any space but must land in a wasteland that is not occupied by enemy pieces, own pieces already in the landing wasteland remain there with their orientations, pieces on the pushed territory do not move with the card, the moved territory gets the orientation required by the board's horizontal-vertical tile pattern, board coordinates and indices remain consistent, and resulting states remain schema-valid.
close_reason: Implemented pure Rod territory pushing

title: Wire Rods move results through app-state confirmation
description: Replace Rods move unavailable results in app-state confirmation with the implemented game-state transition. Acceptance: completed Rods commands for minion movement, piece pushing, and territory pushing update :game on success, rejected commands keep the staged selection with the structured gameplay error, board and piece subscriptions continue deriving from :game, required distance and orientation controls are exposed through the move panel, board clicks feed existing-territory selections, and tests cover success and rejection without duplicating gameplay rules in app_state.
close_reason: Implemented Rod app-state confirmation wiring

title: Add Rods gameplay feature coverage and docs
description: Add Gherkin-style and focused unit coverage for the completed Rods rule slice, and update docs that describe implemented gameplay behavior. Acceptance: feature scenarios cover moving a minion, pushing a piece, pushing a territory, pip-limited distance, blocked full-territory and void destinations, enemy-occupied territory-push rejection, own-wasteland landing behavior, and orientation rules for owned versus enemy pieces. README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the implemented move surface, tests, or verification workflow changes.
close_reason: done

title: Route keyboard shortcut side effects through re-frame boundary
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: app.cljs owns a mutable keyboard-shortcut-listener atom at lines 1179-1180 and install-keyboard-shortcuts! directly adds/removes a window keydown listener at lines 1197-1226. The listener decides shortcut semantics and dispatches app events, while mount! installs it outside the re-frame event/fx layer at lines 1228-1230. This works, but it leaves browser side-effect lifecycle and the visible hotkey command model split across view code instead of an explicit effect boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move global shortcut registration/cleanup into a focused browser effect/controller namespace or re-frame fx-style boundary.
- Keep shortcut definitions data-driven so the command dialog, listener, and smoke expectations share one source.
- Ensure reload/mount does not leak listeners.
- Preserve ?, G, I, Esc, and board-local keyboard behavior.
close_reason: Implemented shared shortcut catalog and re-frame effect listener boundary; tests, release, and smoke pass.

title: Make re-frame event handlers deterministic with injected randomness
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: reg-event-db handlers should be pure app-db transitions, but initialization and draw confirmation can reach default shuffle. src/main/gnostica/app.cljs registers ::initialize with reg-event-db at lines 14-17, app_state/initialize calls game-state/create-game at lines 130-138, and game_state/ordered-deck defaults shuffle-fn to shuffle at lines 164-169. Draw confirmation goes through app_state/confirmed-move-result at lines 1232-1235, while game_state/apply-draw-move defaults shuffle-fn to shuffle at lines 1742-1745. That puts nondeterminism inside db event handling instead of a coeffect/effect boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- No reg-event-db path invokes default randomness implicitly.
- Introduce an explicit cofx/effects boundary, seed, or precomputed deck/shuffle-fn for initialization and discard reshuffles.
- Keep pure game-state helpers usable from tests with explicit shuffle inputs.
- Add tests that prove app event handling is deterministic when given the same injected inputs.
close_reason: Implemented explicit seeded shuffle cofx boundary for initialize and draw confirmation

title: Sun Cup-then-Disc transition is unreachable from browser move selection
description: The docs describe gnostica.game-state/apply-sun-move as the pure Sun facade for Cup followed by optional Disc, including same-created-target shortcuts. The move panel only chooses among :cup, :rod, and :disc and confirmed-move-result dispatches those to apply-cup-move, apply-rod-move, or apply-disc-move. A Sun source can be played as a separate Cup or Disc, but cannot execute the actual ordered Sun rule or its shortcuts from the browser UI. Evidence: README.md line 51, AGENTS.md line 31, MIND_MAP.md node [46], src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc move-power-order and confirmed-move-result, and game_state_test Sun coverage around apply-sun-move. Add Sun-specific staging/confirmation or explicitly mark Sun as pure-only/unavailable in the move panel and docs.
close_reason: Exposed Sun as explicit unavailable browser move power and documented current pure-only status.

title: Wheel Cup creation fails instead of reshuffling an exhausted draw pile
description: docs/rules.txt says to reshuffle the discard pile whenever the draw pile is exhausted, and Wheel creates the new territory from the first card of the draw pile. In src/main/gnostica/game_state/cup.cljc, create-wasteland-territory reads first draw-pile directly for :territory-card-source :draw-pile-top and returns :draw-pile-empty when no draw card exists. It does not accept a shuffle-fn or reshuffle discard into draw first. Reproduced a valid Wheel hand-source Cup territory creation with an empty draw pile and a non-empty discard pile returning :draw-pile-empty. Acceptance: Wheel draw-pile territory creation uses the same deterministic reshuffle boundary as draw actions, has a regression test for empty draw plus discard, and still rejects truly unavailable cards when both piles are empty.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Represent Cup major-arcana variants separately from plain Cup
description: src/main/gnostica/cards.cljc treats :cup-unbounded, :wheel-cup, and :wild-suits as plain Cup sources. The icon guide describes Empress ignoring the three-piece limit and Wheel optionally creating territory from the top draw-pile card, but app-state/apply-cup-move only expose normal Cup placement and hand one-point-card territory creation. Acceptance: carry the selected icon/variant through move commands, support or reject variant-specific options explicitly, and update docs/tests.
close_reason: done

title: Move Three.js runtime detection out of view render path
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: board-stage reads browser globals during view rendering by calling three-board/runtime-status, three-board/three-revision, and three-board/available? at app.cljs lines 464-467. Those helpers read js/THREE and OrbitControls directly in three_board.cljs lines 41-78. Re-frame views should mostly render subscription data; runtime capability probing is an environment/coeffect concern and should not be recomputed inside normal render.

Acceptance criteria:
- Capture Three.js runtime status through an explicit startup/lifecycle event, cofx, or effect boundary.
- Store renderer capability/status in app-db and expose it through subscriptions.
- Keep fallback rendering driven by subscription data rather than direct js global reads in views.
- Preserve current missing-global and revision-mismatch smoke behavior.
close_reason: Moved Three.js runtime detection into app-db board view data

title: Header challenge availability is recomputed in the re-frame subscription
description: src/main/gnostica/app/events.cljs builds :gnostica.app/header-view with an inline can-announce-challenge? check based on current-player, game-status, active challenge, and finished state. app-state/header-view already delegates this to app-state/can-announce-challenge?, which calls game-state/can-announce-challenge? and is the gameplay facade for the rule. The duplicate subscription logic can drift from the real event guard and violates the app-state view-model boundary. Acceptance: make the header-view subscription reuse the same app-state/game-state challenge availability logic as app-state/header-view, and add a test that compares the registered subscription/view-model behavior with the facade for blocked cases.
close_reason: Delegated header-view subscription to app-state/header-view and added facade-alignment coverage for challenge availability.

title: Add hotkey to hide card icons with hover descriptions
description:
Goal: add a keyboard-accessible option to hide the always-visible Gnostica/special-move card icon overlays and instead reveal them on card hover/focus with brief descriptions.

Requirements:
- Add a hotkey or keyboard-accessible toggle that switches card icons between always-visible overlays and hidden-by-default popup mode.
- In popup mode, hovering or focusing a card should show that card special-move icons with concise text descriptions from the shared icon metadata.
- Preserve board, hand, discard, Three.js, and CSS fallback usability; hidden icons should not obscure card art until requested.
- Ensure keyboard users can discover or trigger the same descriptions without relying only on mouse hover.
- Keep icon labels sourced from gnostica.icons rather than duplicating text in renderer-specific code.
- Update tests or smoke coverage for the new mode where practical.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can toggle card icon overlays off via hotkey or equivalent keyboard-accessible control.
- Hovering or focusing a card in the new mode shows the icons and brief descriptions for one-, two-, and three-icon major cards.
- Normal always-visible icon rendering remains available.
- Three.js and CSS fallback paths remain usable, with no incoherent overlap or layout shift.
close_reason: done

title: Implement standalone draw/discard turn transition
description: Add a browser-free gameplay transition for the standalone draw/discard turn option mentioned in README, AGENTS, and MIND_MAP. Acceptance: transition returns structured success/error maps, draws from draw pile into current player's hand or discard depending on rule interpretation from docs/rules.txt, preserves deck accounting and schema invariants, app-state confirmation uses the pure transition instead of an unavailable result, tests cover success and rejection, and docs/mind map are updated.
close_reason: Implemented pure draw/discard transition, wired draw confirmations through app-state, added tests, and updated README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP.

title: Sun shortcuts bypass Cup targeting
description: docs/rules.txt says Sun is Cup followed by Disc, so the Cup half still has to target the minion target space. The pure Sun shortcut paths in src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc create a medium piece or royalty territory without checking that the Cup target is targetable by the acting minion. Repro: a Rose minion at board index 0 facing east can apply hand Sun with cup target board index 8 plus disc created-piece and succeeds, creating :rose-medium-1 at board index 8. A similar created-territory shortcut succeeds at wasteland row 3 col 2 even though the minion target is row 0 col 1. Add target-coordinate validation to both Sun shortcut paths and regression tests for out-of-range territory and wasteland shortcut targets.
close_reason: Validated Sun shortcut Cup target coordinates and added regression coverage.

title: Render first-piece drag source as small pyramid
description:
The Place first piece drag source should look like a small Icehouse pyramid, not a generic diamond/swatch, so the user understands they are dragging a piece onto the board.

Context:
- The current Move panel source uses a small colored diamond next to Place first piece.
- This should visually read as the current player's small pyramid before the drag starts.
- Related UX: bd-3qj5 covers rendering a piece ghost under the pointer while dragging.

Acceptance criteria:
- The Place first piece icon is shaped like a small pyramid/cone consistent with board pieces.
- The icon uses the current player's color.
- The source remains keyboard/click accessible and draggable when direct manipulation is enabled.
- Disabled state remains clear when the player cannot place an initial piece.
close_reason: Implemented first-piece source as a current-player small pyramid icon and verified via smoke coverage.

title: Render and expose pieces that occupy wasteland spaces
description: Gameplay can place and move pieces into wasteland spaces, for example initial placement can create {:space {:kind :wasteland ...}}. The board renderers only group and render pieces by :space-index: gnostica.pieces/pieces-by-space groups wasteland pieces under nil, the Three.js scene only adds piece meshes when a cell exists for (:space-index piece), and the CSS fallback only draws markers on board-card elements. Result: a legal wasteland piece is present in board-pieces but invisible and hard to inspect. Acceptance: represent board and wasteland piece occupancy by stable space coordinates, render wasteland piece markers/meshes in both 3D and CSS fallback paths, expose them in relevant panel data, and add tests/smoke coverage for initial placement or Rod movement into a wasteland.
close_reason: Implemented stable territory/wasteland piece space keys, rendered wasteland pieces in Three.js and CSS fallback, and added unit plus smoke coverage.

title: Expose full Devil multi-orient staging or mark omitted steps explicitly
description:
Pure apply-devil-move supports up to three ordered :orient-target actions and retargets after each action. Browser move selection currently treats Devil like a single target-piece plus orientation command through devil-requirements and piece-orientation-command, so there is no browser path to collect a second or third Devil orientation even though README, MIND_MAP, and the pure feature scenario describe retargeted Devil behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser staging either supports one, two, or three Devil orientation actions with retargeting after each staged action, or explicitly presents the extra Devil actions as unavailable/deferred instead of silently omitting them.
- Confirmed Devil browser commands use :actions when multiple orientations are staged.
- App-state tests cover at least a two-step retargeting Devil browser command, or the explicit unavailable behavior.
close_reason: Implemented multi-step Devil browser staging with regression coverage.

title: Require acting minion piece-id in suit command schemas
description: The new command schemas use one shared MoveSource where :piece-id is optional for both territory and hand-card sources. That is correct for some major source contracts, but it makes suit commands structurally valid without an acting minion even though Cup/Rod/Disc/Sword public contracts and resolvers require one. Evidence: (valid-command? :cup {:player-id :rose :source {:kind :hand-card :card-id "cups2"} :target {:kind :territory :board-index 1} :orientation :north}) returns true, then the real transition fails later with :invalid-piece. Acceptance: introduce suit-specific source schemas that require :piece-id for Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, Sun subcommands where an acting minion is required, while preserving major-source schemas that intentionally omit it; add negative schema tests for missing acting minions on suit commands.

title: Remove or document stale bd embedded-Dolt artifacts after br migration
description: The current repo docs and MIND_MAP say to use br/beads_rust, and br doctor reports a healthy 190-record store, but the legacy bd command still auto-discovers .beads and fails on bd list --json because .beads/embeddeddolt/beads/.dolt/repo_state.json is missing. The stale embedded-Dolt artifacts plus .beads/config.yaml can mislead agents or contributors into using the broken legacy tool. Acceptance: either remove/ignore the stale bd embedded-Dolt state and make the repo clearly br-only, or add a short migration note explaining that bd is intentionally unsupported here; br list/ready/doctor/sync should remain clean.
close_reason: Documented the br-only issue tracking boundary, marked legacy bd embedded-Dolt state unsupported, and removed the ignored local embedded-Dolt cache.

title: Fool revealed-card play omits major powers
description: docs/rules.txt says Fool turns over the next draw card and plays it, optionally repeating once. It does not limit revealed play to minor suit powers. In src/main/gnostica/game_state/draw.cljc, apply-fool-play only accepts :cup, :rod, :disc, and :sword, rejecting other revealed powers with :invalid-fool-play-power. That leaves revealed major cards unplayable through the pure Fool transition even when their normal browser-free facades exist. Acceptance: Fool delegation supports implemented revealed major powers by routing through the same major/suit facades used elsewhere, preserves the single Fool source charge and card accounting, and has regression coverage for at least one non-suit revealed major plus the existing suit cases.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Define machine-checkable command contracts for gameplay facades
description: Public facades in src/main/gnostica/game_state.cljc and the underlying rule namespaces rely on large prose contracts in AGENTS.md/README plus ad hoc map destructuring and resolver checks. Malli coverage in src/main/gnostica/game_schema.cljc focuses on state shape, not command shape. As command maps grow for World, Sun, Fool, Moon, composite majors, and discard-pile variants, invalid or ambiguous command shapes are harder to diagnose and browser builders have no shared contract to target. Acceptance: add machine-checkable schemas or validators for the public command maps and structured error shape, at least for the high-variance move families. Use them in tests and/or resolver entry points without coupling gameplay code to UI state.
close_reason: Implemented command schema contracts and tests

title: Separate move command construction from confirmation side effects
description: src/main/gnostica/move_selection/commands.cljc now owns both command construction and confirmation/application behavior: confirmed-move-result dispatches to game-state facades, apply-confirmed-move-result mutates app-db keys such as :game, :turn-action, and :move-selection, and confirm-move handles staged rejection. That makes a namespace named commands responsible for app-state transition policy as well as command shapes, weakening the boundary between pure command builders and browser/app-db confirmation flow. Evidence: commands.cljc around 325-472. Acceptance: keep command builders in a focused namespace and move transition dispatch plus app-db result application into the facade or a clearly named confirmation namespace; preserve current public move-selection API and add focused tests around rejected confirmations and turn-action consumption.

title: Implement Coins/Discs gameplay rules
description: Add pure gameplay support for Coins/Discs moves in gnostica.game-state and the shared confirmation path. Scope covers growing a target piece by exactly one size and growing a target territory by replacing it with a card worth exactly one more point, using docs/rules.txt as the local rule authority. Acceptance: transitions return explicit success/failure maps, preserve schema, deck, board, and piece/stash invariants, append history events, reject missing stash/replacement resources, enforce enemy-occupied territory restrictions, handle hand-card discard behavior, and keep renderer-specific work out of the gameplay layer.
close_reason: All Coins/Discs implementation, app wiring, variant, feature coverage, and docs subtasks are closed; clojure -M:test passes

title: Define Coins/Discs command contract and card helpers
description: Introduce the browser-free command/action shape and reusable legality helpers for Coins/Discs. Acceptance: commands explicitly identify player id, source as territory or hand card, acting minion piece id, target as piece or territory, selected disc variant, optional replacement-card source, optional replacement card id, and optional orientation when growing the current player's piece. Card helpers identify Coins minor cards plus Disc-bearing majors such as Magician, Strength, Star, and Sun; target resolution follows docs/rules.txt targeting rules for upright/current-space, cardinal adjacent-space, and minion self-targeting; invalid commands return structured errors without mutating game state.
close_reason: Implemented Disc command contract and card helpers

title: Implement Coins/Discs piece growth
description: Implement the Coins/Discs effect that grows the acting minion or a legal target piece by exactly one size. Acceptance: small pieces become medium and medium pieces become large, large pieces are rejected, the larger replacement must be available in the same owner's stash, the old piece returns to that owner's stash, player and pieces stash mirrors stay consistent, current-player grown pieces may receive a validated orientation, enemy grown pieces preserve orientation, hand-source Coins cards move to discard, history events are appended, and resulting states remain schema-valid.
close_reason: Implemented pure Coins/Discs piece growth

title: Implement Coins/Discs territory growth
description: Implement the Coins/Discs effect that grows a legal target territory. Acceptance: target territories occupied by enemy pieces are rejected, the existing territory card moves to discard, the replacement card must be worth exactly one more point than the original card, spot cards can grow to royalty cards and royalty cards can grow to major arcana, the replacement normally comes from the current player's hand, pieces on the target coordinate stay in place with their orientations intact, board orientation remains compatible with the board pattern, unique card accounting is preserved, hand-source Coins cards move to discard without duplicating replacement cards, history events are appended, and resulting states remain schema-valid.
close_reason: done

title: Wire Coins/Discs through move selection and app-state confirmation
description: Replace completed Coins/Discs selections in app-state confirmation with the implemented game-state transition instead of :move-transition-unavailable. Acceptance: move-selection exposes Disc as a selectable power when a source card provides it, stages piece versus territory growth controls, offers target-piece and target-territory choices that match targeting rules, offers replacement-card and replacement-source controls for territory growth, carries the selected disc variant into apply-disc/apply-coins move commands, successful confirmations update :game, rejected confirmations keep the staged selection with structured gameplay errors, and tests cover success and rejection without duplicating gameplay rules in app_state.
close_reason: Implemented Disc move-selection and app-state confirmation

title: Support Coins/Discs major-arcana variants and shortcuts
description: Add the Disc-related major-card behavior on top of the core Coins/Discs transition. Acceptance: Magician can choose a wild-suit Disc variant; Strength can apply up to two Disc actions and can skip the intermediate piece size or territory value when both actions grow the same target; Star performs the orient-minion step before Disc and may source territory-growth replacement cards from anywhere in the discard pile, including the just-discarded Star when played from hand; Sun can apply Cup then Disc in rule order and can skip the intermediate step when both powers affect the same newly-created target; all variants preserve explicit success/error maps, history, discard behavior, and schema/deck invariants.
close_reason: done

title: Add Coins/Discs gameplay feature coverage and docs
description: Add Gherkin-style and focused unit coverage for the completed Coins/Discs rule slice, and update docs that describe implemented gameplay behavior. Acceptance: feature scenarios cover piece growth, territory growth, owned versus enemy orientation rules, missing stash rejection, large-piece rejection, missing replacement-card rejection, enemy-occupied territory rejection, hand-source discard behavior, Star discard-pile replacement behavior, and Strength or Sun shortcut behavior where implemented. README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the implemented move surface, tests, or verification workflow changes; clojure -M:test passes.
close_reason: Added Disc Gherkin coverage and synchronized docs; clojure -M:test passes

title: Draw/discard move panel cannot select discards at a full hand
description: README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md describe the standalone draw/discard turn as calling apply-draw-move, including optional discard-card-ids and drawing after selected discards. The browser move-selection state only requires draw-count, has no event/control path for selecting discard-card-ids, and max-draw-count ignores cards the player could discard. Result: at the normal six-card hand, the Draw cards source is disabled, so the UI cannot perform the basic rules action to discard any or none and then draw. Evidence: README.md lines 43, 45, and 61. AGENTS.md lines 35 and 47. src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc move-source-definitions, max-draw-count, draw-count-options, and move-command. Add discard-card selection state, controls, command building, and tests, or narrow the docs if this remains intentionally pure-only.
close_reason: Implemented draw discard-card selection in the move panel

title: Keep Sun Cup target-piece controls visible after Disc mode selection
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs:824 hides the Cup target-piece chooser once :sun-disc-mode is set. Sun can Cup onto a piece and then skip or perform a Disc follow-up, but after choosing the Disc mode the original Cup target-piece selection disappears and the remaining target-piece control belongs to the Disc step. Acceptance: keep a distinct Cup target-piece summary/control visible throughout the Sun flow, keep Disc target controls separate, and add view-model or UI coverage for piece Cup target plus skip and piece Cup target plus Disc piece.
close_reason: Fixed Sun Cup target-piece summary during Disc staging

title: Separate smoke runner CDP harness from Gnostica assertions
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: test/gnostica/smoke_runner.clj is 1125 lines and combines Chrome process management, DevTools protocol transport, JS snippets, screenshot analysis, input dispatch, server startup, and Gnostica board assertions. The Gnostica-specific scenario in run-happy-viewport! around lines 929-1032 sits on top of a reusable browser harness, but both layers are in one namespace.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract CDP/Chrome lifecycle, command/evaluate/wait/input helpers into a test support namespace.
- Move page-stat JavaScript snippets and readiness predicates into focused Gnostica smoke modules or data structures.
- Leave smoke_runner.clj as high-level scenario orchestration.
- Preserve current clojure -M:smoke behavior and diagnostics.
close_reason: done

title: Harden pinned CDN script loading for Three.js
description:
Problem: `src/main/resources/index.html` loads Three.js and OrbitControls from pinned jsDelivr URLs at runtime, but there is no subresource integrity, crossorigin policy, local fallback, or automated check that the fetched bytes still match the expected r128 global build. The app has a CSS fallback when globals are absent, but release reproducibility and offline/corporate-network behavior still depend on a third-party CDN.

Evidence:
- `index.html` references `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.128.0/...` directly before `/js/main.js`.
- `src/main/externs/three.ext.js` is tied to the r128 global API, so accidental CDN or add-on drift can compile successfully but fail at runtime.

Acceptance criteria:
- Choose a hardened loading strategy that preserves the npm-free workflow: SRI plus crossorigin, vendored pinned scripts, or a documented local fallback path.
- Verify Three.js and OrbitControls are on the same r128 release line and fail visibly if a mismatched global is loaded.
- Add or update an automated/smoke check that detects missing or mismatched CDN globals before user interaction.
- Document the chosen strategy in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
- `clojure -M:release` succeeds after the change with advanced-compilation externs still aligned.
close_reason: Added SRI/crossorigin to pinned Three.js CDN scripts, gated the runtime on r128 plus OrbitControls, added missing and mismatched global smoke coverage, and documented the strategy.

title: Show unselected orientation controls as none instead of Unknown
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs:490 renders Current using pieces/orientation-label even when no orientation has been selected, and src/main/gnostica/pieces.cljc returns Unknown for nil. In move staging this reads like an error state rather than an unchosen optional value. Acceptance: unselected orientation controls display a neutral value such as None or Choose orientation while truly invalid orientation values can still surface as errors elsewhere.
close_reason: Implemented: unselected move-panel orientation controls now show None while invalid orientation labels still surface as Unknown. Verified with clojure -M:test and clojure -M:release.

title: Direct move-entry previews and orientation compass are not implemented
description: Direct move-entry spec and closed tickets bd-5h4.4 and bd-5h4.7 describe ghost previews, snapped Rod or Hermit path previews, distance and destination feedback, and a compass or ring for orientation. Current renderer preview is a text summary from three_board.cljs drag-preview-summary backed by pointer.cljs target descriptor status; no path, mutation preview, distance visual, or orientation compass/ring is implemented, and docs/direct_move_entry_spec.md still lists preview and compass work as an implementation phase. Acceptance: either implement the visual preview and compass behavior in 3D and CSS fallback with tests or smoke coverage, or explicitly mark it deferred and split remaining tickets so the direct manipulation epic status matches shipped behavior.
notes: Implemented shared move-preview data, CSS fallback spatial overlays, Three.js preview overlay, orientation compass callbacks, Disc/Sword/Sun mutation summaries, app-state tests, docs, and mind-map update. Verified with clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke.
close_reason: Implemented shared direct move previews and orientation compass rendering with tests and smoke verification.

title: Extract shared spatial and target helpers for game-state rules
description: Several pure rule namespaces define near-identical helpers for coordinates, target summaries, discard-pile lookups, and piece movement: coordinate-map appears in cup.cljc, rod.cljc, disc.cljc, sword.cljc, manipulation.cljc, and composite.cljc; same-coordinate? appears in rod/disc/sword/manipulation/composite and move_selection; discard-pile-card appears in major/cup/rod/disc/sword; move-piece-to-space appears in rod/manipulation/composite. The duplication makes subtle targeting or accounting differences hard to spot. Acceptance: add shared pure helper namespaces for spatial lookup/target summaries/source-card lookup or otherwise consolidate these helpers. Keep local variants only when the semantic difference is named and tested, and keep clojure -M:test passing.
close_reason: Consolidated shared spatial and discard-card helpers across pure game-state rules; added direct spatial helper tests; clojure -M:test and clojure-lsp diagnostics pass.

title: Shuffle the deck before dealing table cards
description:
Board setup currently deals the first nine cards from the ordered deck into the 3x3 territory grid. Add a shuffle step before dealing cards onto the table so each new table uses a randomized deck order.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shuffle the tarot deck before selecting the nine table/territory cards.
- Preserve existing board invariants: nine face-up cards, 3x3 placement, and alternating portrait/landscape orientations.
- Keep the shuffle path testable, preferably by injecting or seeding randomness instead of hard-coding global random state.
- Update or add tests that prove the deal no longer depends on the unshuffled deck order while the existing layout rules still hold.
close_reason: done

title: Fade board velvet into black beyond wastelands
description:
Adjust the board background so the velvet colour under and around territory cards fades to black with distance from the playable area. The velvet should remain visible under the territory cards and wasteland outlines, but by the time the surface extends past the wasteland squares and roughly halfway into the void it should be black.

Acceptance criteria:
- Three.js board rendering uses a distance-based fade or equivalent material/texture so the central territory area remains velvet and the outer void reaches black at the specified distance.
- CSS fallback board background matches the same visual intent closely enough that wasteland squares sit on velvet and farther void space reads as black.
- The fade respects current board-space/wasteland layout helpers instead of hard-coding only the initial 3x3 board dimensions where avoidable.
- Territory cards, wasteland outlines, pieces, card selection highlights, and icon popovers remain readable against the darker outer surface.
- Desktop and mobile smoke checks continue to see nonblank board content and dark table/void pixels; update smoke expectations if existing pixel thresholds assume uniform velvet.
- Update MIND_MAP.md or README.md if renderer behavior or smoke assertions change materially.
close_reason: Implemented the Three.js and CSS board fade, updated smoke checks and docs.

title: Tighten game schema invariants for impossible gameplay states
description: The Malli GameState schema currently accepts several states that the pure gameplay contract says should be impossible. Confirmed examples: a piece map with both :space-index and :space validates because Piece is an open :or map; a piece in {:space {:kind :wasteland :row 99 :col 99}} validates even when that coordinate is not a current wasteland; and [:setup :target-score] accepts any positive int such as 99 despite create-game allowing only 8, 9, or 10. Acceptance: require exactly one piece location shape, validate wasteland piece coordinates against board-layout/wasteland-spaces, restrict target score to game-state/allowed-target-scores, and surface specific invariant errors for these cases.
close_reason: Implemented schema invariants for piece locations, wasteland coordinates, and official target scores.

title: Restore clojure-lsp diagnostics after board drag-hover changes
description: A fresh clojure-lsp diagnostics run exits nonzero even though clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke pass. Current diagnostics report unresolved symbols in src/main/gnostica/ui/board.cljs around the r/with-let drag-hover/finally locals, plus unused bindings in app_state.cljc and move_selection.cljc. Acceptance: make clojure-lsp diagnostics exit zero again, either by rewriting the board-stage lifecycle shape so the analyzer sees the bindings, configuring an intentional analyzer limitation, and removing the real unused bindings; keep release and smoke green.
close_reason: Fixed clojure-lsp diagnostics; verified diagnostics, tests, release, and smoke

title: Map gesture pending alternatives to concrete missing choices
description: The pending gesture tray exposes raw move-selection requirements instead of the concrete choice the user must make. Evidence: move-selection can report internal missing fields such as :target-resolution, :sun-disc-target-piece-id, :sun-disc-target-board-index, and :sun-disc-replacement-card-id. gesture-intent/alternative-options handles :target-resolution by always returning orientation options and lets the Sun-specific fields fall through to empty options, so the tray can show a generic prompt with wrong or empty alternatives while Detailed entry has the real stage. Acceptance: derive pending-tray alternatives from the same concrete stage mapping used by move-selection, or add explicit alternatives for target-resolution and Sun-specific requirements; cover Cup territory, Cup wasteland, and Sun Disc modes.
close_reason: Mapped pending gesture alternatives to concrete choices with Cup and Sun coverage

title: Direct move-entry smoke does not exercise real touch input
description: Closed direct manipulation verification ticket bd-5h4.9 asked for mobile/touch viewport coverage for at least one card drag and one piece drag. Current smoke runs a mobile viewport, but the gesture probes are mouse or synthetic only: browser dispatch-drag uses CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent, hand-card-drag-js and fallback-piece-drag-js dispatch DragEvent dragstart directly, and there is no touchStart, pointerType touch, or long-press path. This can pass while touch or pen direct gestures are broken. Acceptance: add a mobile touch or pointer smoke path, or document touch as deferred, that exercises at least one card and one piece gesture through real touch or pointer input and fails if pending tray or Detailed entry cannot be reached.
close_reason: Added mobile touch smoke coverage for hand-card and Three.js piece gestures

title: Clarify AGENTS test command comment includes feature scenarios
description: AGENTS.md:15 says clojure -M:test runs Clojure tests for shared code, while AGENTS.md:45 and README.md:91 say the same command also runs custom Gherkin-style gameplay feature scenarios. The command summary should not make feature failures look outside the normal test path. Acceptance: update the command comment to match README.md and test/gnostica/test_runner.clj.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md test command comment to include gameplay feature scenarios.

title: Implement Cup enemy-piece creation targets
description: docs/rules.txt Cups section allows a Cup to create an enemy small piece when targeting an enemy piece, preserving the enemy piece orientation. apply-cup-move only supports creating the current player's small piece on a target territory and its command target has no piece-target variant. README.md and AGENTS.md document the narrower behavior, so the current Cup slice omits a legal Cup branch. Acceptance: add explicit command/UI support and tests, or document the branch as unsupported in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
close_reason: Implemented Cup enemy-piece creation targets

title: First-piece drag highlights every placement area instead of only hovered target
description:
Actual: While dragging the first small piece for initial placement, every legal empty territory and wasteland is highlighted at once.

Expected: During a first-piece drag, only the wasteland or territory that would be selected if the drag were released at the current pointer location should show the active drag highlight. Other legal destinations may remain eligible through descriptors, but they should not all render as highlighted drag targets.

Repro notes: Start a normal local game from the lobby so the current player has no pieces on the board. Begin dragging the first/initial placement piece over the board. All empty territories and wastelands highlight instead of just the current drop target.

Scope: Direct manipulation initial placement highlighting in both Three.js and CSS fallback paths. Keep legal-target descriptors available for hit testing, preserve pending tray/Detailed entry fallback behavior, and avoid mutating :game before confirmation.

Related: follow-up to bd-2vcc, which restored source-only first-piece drag target descriptors but made all legal destinations visibly highlighted.
close_reason: Fixed first-piece drag highlighting so only the hovered territory or wasteland renders as the active drag target while other legal destinations remain available for hit testing, with CSS and Three.js smoke coverage.

title: Render Gnostica icons on major arcana cards

The PDF describes 22 icon triplets for the major arcana, placed on the upper-left corner of the correct major card. title: Reduce move-panel duplication of browser-free move-selection state
description:
src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs duplicates state-machine decisions from src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc, including composite/sword major active-action functions and large per-power control case tables. This weakens cohesion because the UI infers the active action phase from :power and :major-actions instead of rendering explicit control metadata from the browser-free move-selection/app-state view model. Any future change to ordered major staging must be updated in multiple places.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move active action/control metadata into gnostica.move-selection or the app-state move-panel view model.
- Keep gnostica.ui.move-panel mostly declarative over view-model control groups.
- Remove duplicate composite-active-action-power and sword-major-active-action-power logic from the UI namespace.
- Add focused tests that view-model control groups match staged powers for representative composite, Sword-major, Sun, and World flows.
close_reason: Implemented move-panel control groups in browser-free move selection, refactored UI rendering, and added focused view-model tests.

title: Track browser move-selection gaps for implemented major powers
description: Pure browser-free facades now exist for Fool, High Priestess, Judgement, World, and the ordered multi-action/composite major flows, but the browser move-selection power list only exposes :cup, :rod, :disc, :sun, :sword, :hierophant, :hermit, and :devil. Some major cards are usable only as single suit subsets, and World/draw-major powers are unavailable from the browser despite implemented pure transitions. Acceptance: either add a clear browser-support epic with child tickets for these remaining staged controls or wire the missing flows directly; keep explicit unavailable results for powers still intentionally unwired so implemented pure rules do not disappear from product planning.
close_reason: Tracked remaining browser support under bd-ydt with child tickets for draw majors, ordered composite suit majors, Sword-major composites, World delegation, and power-choice/unavailable-state audit.

title: Split app.cljs browser shell into focused view modules
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: src/main/gnostica/app.cljs is 1263 lines and acts as re-frame registration, CSS fallback board renderer, shared card renderer, card zones, territory panel, move panel, help dialogs, keyboard shortcuts, startup, and smoke init hook. The first 300 lines are event/sub registration, while later sections contain board rendering, move controls, dialogs, global key handling, and init logic. Since domain state is already delegated to app_state and 3D rendering to three_board, this namespace is carrying too many browser responsibilities.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move board fallback/card-zone/territory-panel/move-panel/help-dialog views into focused UI namespaces.
- Keep re-frame event/sub registration either in a thin wiring namespace or colocated with clear feature boundaries.
- Keep gnostica.app as the browser shell/startup entry point.
- Preserve existing subscriptions/events and smoke-visible DOM attributes.
close_reason: done

title: Add gameplay move selection controls
description:
Goal: build the user interface flow that lets a player choose and confirm legal gameplay moves once the pure gameplay state and rule transitions are available.

Requirements:
- Add a move-selection mode to the app UI that is driven by shared gameplay state rather than renderer-only board state.
- Let the current player choose the move source, such as activating a board territory, playing a hand card, drawing cards, orienting a piece, or placing an initial small piece when they have no pieces on the board.
- Guide the player through required targets and parameters for the selected move: card, piece/minion, target space or piece, orientation, hand-card choice, draw count, and confirmation/cancel states as applicable.
- Surface only currently legal choices when the rule engine can provide them, and show clear disabled/error states for rejected transitions.
- Keep Three.js board selection, CSS fallback selection, territory panel, and future hand/player panels synchronized through re-frame events/subscriptions.
- Make the flow keyboard and pointer accessible enough for repeated gameplay, with predictable focus and no reliance on hover-only controls.
- Preserve deterministic, testable boundaries: UI events should call pure transition helpers and render their explicit success/error results.

Acceptance criteria:
- The UI has a clear current-player move picker and staged selection controls for source, target, options, confirm, and cancel.
- Selecting board cards and pieces feeds the active move flow instead of only updating the inspection panel.
- Invalid or incomplete selections cannot silently mutate state; errors are visible and recoverable.
- Tests cover the re-frame event/subscription state machine where practical, and manual smoke notes cover board/card/piece selection integration.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if new UI state namespaces, commands, or gameplay verification steps are added.

Implementation notes:
- This should follow the shared gameplay state/player setup/rule-transition work closely enough that the UI can consume authoritative move legality instead of duplicating rules.
- Prefer small reusable controls for move source selection, target prompts, orientation selection, and confirmation actions.
close_reason: Implemented move-selection UI flow with app-state tests, docs, release build, and smoke verification.

title: Recompress MIND_MAP after recent expansions
description:
MIND_MAP.md line 3 asks agents to keep the map compact (20-50 nodes typical) and line-oriented, but the file now has 53 nodes and several very large single-line nodes, especially [24], [29], [37], [38], [46], and [47]. This makes grep output and review diffs hard to use. A previous compaction ticket was closed before the latest expansions, so this is current drift rather than an open duplicate.

Acceptance: consolidate, split, or rebalance nodes so the map stays within the stated compact target or deliberately revise the target; keep [6-10] as practical anchors, preserve [1-5], and reduce extreme node lengths while retaining useful cross-links.
close_reason: Recompressed MIND_MAP.md to 50 nodes and shortened oversized nodes.

title: Add local lobby for player setup before game start
description:
Add a first-screen local lobby flow before creating the game. This is scoped to the current single-browser app: players join locally, enter a display name, choose an available player colour, and then start the game. Networked multiplayer sync remains out of scope unless a separate ticket adds it.

Acceptance criteria:
- App initialization can start in a lobby state without immediately creating :game.
- Lobby UI lets two to six players join, edit their display names, choose unique colours from gnostica.pieces/players metadata, remove joined players before start, and start only when setup is valid.
- Starting the game calls the existing gnostica.game-state/create-game path with the selected player ids/names while preserving deterministic shuffle injection through the app event/cofx boundary.
- Existing demo/smoke init options can still bypass or prefill the lobby so current verification flows remain deterministic.
- Header, board, card-zone, territory, and move-panel views handle the pre-game lobby state without nil-game errors.
- Add app-state and UI-facing tests for lobby validation, duplicate colour rejection, player count limits, name persistence, start-game initialization, and cancellation/removal behavior.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the startup flow, app-db shape, or smoke workflow changes.
close_reason: Implemented in the current codebase and verified with clojure -M:test.

title: Harden create-game validation for player and deck inputs
description:
Problem: `gnostica.game-state/create-game` validates player count, missing ids, duplicate ids, and minimum deck size, but it accepts player ids with no known piece/player metadata and accepts any deck-like collection with at least nine entries. That leaves the authoritative state boundary open to malformed states before Malli/schema work exists. It also exposes a rule mismatch: setup claims two to six players, while `gnostica.pieces/players` currently defines only four color/player records.

Evidence:
- `normalize-player` merges optional `pieces/players-by-id` data and then the caller supplied player spec, so unknown ids can enter state with incomplete color metadata.
- `validate-deck` checks only `(count deck) >= board/board-card-count`, not uniqueness or card shape.
- Existing tests reject too few or too many players but do not cover unknown ids, five/six-player metadata, duplicate cards, or malformed deck entries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide and enforce whether player ids must be from known player metadata or whether custom player metadata is required for unknown ids.
- Two-through-six-player setup either has complete player/color metadata for every legal player count or returns structured validation errors explaining the unsupported players.
- Deck validation catches duplicate card ids and malformed/missing card data needed by setup and rendering.
- Validation failures use the existing explicit result map shape with stable error codes and useful `:data`.
- Tests cover unknown player ids, five/six-player setup behavior, duplicate deck entries, malformed cards, and the existing happy path.
close_reason: Hardened create-game player/deck validation, added six player metadata, expanded tests, and documented the stricter boundary.

title: Drive browser state from the shared game-state contract
description:
Problem: README.md and AGENTS.md identify `gnostica.game-state/create-game` as the authoritative browser-free gameplay state boundary, but `gnostica.app/::initialize` still builds a separate renderer-oriented state with `board/initial-board`, `pieces/initial-pieces`, and `:selected-board-index`. That creates a second setup path that bypasses explicit success/error results, player metadata, turn order, draw/discard state, setup/bid metadata, and future rule transitions.

Evidence:
- `src/main/gnostica/app.cljs` initializes top-level `:board` and `:pieces` directly.
- `src/main/gnostica/game_state.cljc` already provides deterministic `create-game` results intended for re-frame and tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser initialization creates and stores an authoritative game state through `gnostica.game-state/create-game` for the default/demo players, or renders an explicit setup error if creation fails.
- Board, piece, selected territory, and current-player subscriptions derive from the shared game state rather than parallel renderer-only top-level state.
- Any demo-only visible piece fixtures are either represented through the gameplay state contract or isolated and named as non-game fixtures.
- Focused tests cover the initialization path and at least one selection/update path against the new state shape.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the app-db shape or verification workflow changes.
close_reason: Browser app initialization now stores the shared create-game state under :game, derives board/piece/current-player subscriptions from it, covers the app-db path with focused tests, and updates README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP.

title: Players with no board pieces can end turn before mandatory placement
description: docs/rules.txt says a player with no pieces on the board may only put a small piece on any empty territory or wasteland. The shared end-turn facade in src/main/gnostica/game_state/core.cljc does not enforce that no-piece restriction, and header state exposes end turn whenever the game is active and current player exists. Reproduced a fresh game with no pieces where gnostica.game-state/end-turn for :rose succeeded and advanced to :indigo. Acceptance: a current player with no active board or wasteland pieces must perform apply-initial-placement before ending the turn, browser controls should reflect that restriction, and regression tests cover the fresh-game case plus normal end-turn after placement.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Make High Priestess browser redraw staging sequential
description:
Browser staging validates both High Priestess redraw passes against the original post-source hand instead of the hand after prior staged passes. high-priestess-hand-card-options, high-priestess-valid-discard-card-ids, and the draw-count options do not simulate pass 1 before pass 2.
