The UI can stage pass 1 discard cups2 draw 0 and pass 2 discard cups2 draw 0 as :confirm; apply-high-priestess-move then rejects with :invalid-discard-cards. Conversely, pass 2 cannot select cards drawn in pass 1.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each redraw pass derives discard and draw options from the state after source cost and all earlier staged redraw passes.
- The same card cannot be discarded after it already left the hand unless it has actually been redrawn.
- Pass 2 support for newly drawn cards is implemented, or the limitation is explicitly represented in the staging UX and command validation.
- App-state tests cover duplicate discard prevention and the chosen behavior for cards drawn during pass 1.
close_reason: Implemented sequential High Priestess redraw staging with app-state coverage.

title: Add feature scenarios for Cup and Sword rule regressions
description:
The feature suite covers setup, Rod, Disc, draw majors, manipulation majors, and endgame, while core Cup and Sword behavior is covered mostly by unit/app-state tests. The missing feature layer made it easier for a Cup target-range regression to survive: there is no Gherkin scenario that says a Cup can only affect the minion target space. Sword has broad unit coverage but no feature-level rule narrative for attack/replacement/destruction flows.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add features/cups_moves.feature covering target range, enemy-piece creation, wasteland territory creation, full territory rejection, and key variants such as Wheel or Empress.
- Add features/swords_moves.feature covering piece attack, territory attack, replacement rejection, destruction/void handling, and at least one Tower or Death scenario.
- Step definitions reuse test/gnostica/feature_world.clj helpers and preserve Malli validation checks.
- clojure -M:test includes the new scenarios.
close_reason: Added Cup and Sword Gherkin feature scenarios covering target range, variants, attacks, replacements, destruction, void handling, and Tower discard-pile replacement.

title: Initial piece drag does not highlight placement destinations
description:
Actual: When the current player has no pieces and drags the first small piece for initial placement, the board does not highlight any legal destination.

Expected: While dragging the first piece, legal empty territories and legal wasteland destinations should be highlighted using the same move-legal-target descriptors used by Detailed entry, Three.js, and the CSS fallback.

Repro notes: Start a normal local game from the lobby so the current player has no pieces on the board. Drag the first/initial placement piece toward the board. No destination highlight appears before drop.

Scope: Direct manipulation initial placement highlighting. Check both Three.js and CSS fallback paths, and keep the pending tray/Detailed entry fallback behavior intact.

Related docs: README.md Gameplay Move Selection, AGENTS.md move-selection guidance, MIND_MAP.md nodes [14], [24], [37], [43], and [51].
close_reason: Fixed source-only initial-placement stash drags so they keep all empty territory and wasteland legal-target descriptors active for board highlighting; added app-state regression coverage and verified test, release, and smoke.

title: Return pieces to stash when territory relocation leaves them in the void
description: Rod territory pushes and Hermit territory moves leave source-coordinate pieces behind by converting pieces on the moved territory to {:space {:kind :wasteland :row ... :col ...}} before moving the card. Unlike Sword territory destruction, these paths do not re-check whether that old coordinate is still a wasteland after the card moves. In a disconnected or gapped board, moving a territory from [0,0] to a wasteland at [0,2] adjacent to another territory can leave the old [0,0] coordinate in the void while the piece remains active. Acceptance: after any territory relocation, return pieces at coordinates that are no longer a territory or current wasteland to their owner stashes, keep stash mirrors reconciled, and cover both Rod territory push and Hermit territory move.
close_reason: done

title: Change board ground to a velvet color
description:
Goal: make the surface beneath the territory cards feel more like a velvet play mat instead of a neutral ground.

Requirements:
- Change the 3D board ground/table surface under the cards to a velvet-toned color.
- Preserve card readability, piece contrast, lighting balance, and existing board spacing.
- Keep the change visual-only: do not alter board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.
- Consider whether the CSS fallback board background should use a matching velvet color for consistency.
- Avoid adding image or texture dependencies unless a flat material cannot produce the desired result.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D board renders with a clearly velvet-colored ground beneath the cards.
- Cards and Icehouse pieces remain legible against the new surface.
- Existing tests and release compilation still pass.
- A smoke or screenshot-based check covers the changed board background if practical.
close_reason: Implemented burgundy velvet 3D and fallback board ground with smoke coverage.

title: Render and target all pieces in unbounded spaces
description: Frontend renderers still cap visible occupants at three via src/main/gnostica/board_layout.cljc:134 and src/main/gnostica/ui/board.cljs:329 even though Empress and Emperor style unbounded powers can legally create spaces with more than three pieces. Fourth and later pieces remain in game state but are invisible on both Three.js and CSS fallback boards, and likely unavailable as direct visual targets. Acceptance: render or summarize overflow occupants in both board renderers, keep every occupant selectable from the board or an adjacent UI, and add a regression fixture/test for a more-than-three-piece space.
close_reason: Rendered all overflow board pieces in Three.js and CSS fallback, kept all occupants in legal-target descriptors, added overflow fixture/regression coverage, and verified clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke.

title: Sun created-target shortcuts skip Cup targeting validation
description: docs/rules.txt makes Sun a Cup followed by Disc, so same-created-target shortcuts still need the Cup target to be in the acting minion target space. In src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc, apply-sun-created-piece-shortcut and apply-sun-created-territory-shortcut validate resources and destination shape but never check the target coordinate against the minion. Reproduced apply-sun-move succeeding with a minion at board index 0 facing east while creating a medium piece at board index 8, and also creating a royalty territory at wasteland row 3 col 2. Acceptance: both Sun shortcut paths reuse the same Cup target validation as normal Cup, add regression tests for out-of-range territory and wasteland shortcuts, and keep valid shortcut examples passing.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Use board cell indexes instead of vector positions in app-state and move-selection
description: Fresh-eye review found that app-state and move-selection often treat :board as a positional vector with get/contains? even though gameplay cells carry stable :index values. Sword territory destruction removes cells and leaves index gaps, so selecting board index 5 after index 4 is destroyed returns the cell with :index 6; higher valid indexes can be rejected entirely. Affects selected-board-cell/select-board-card, move-selection target/source lookup, piece coordinate labels, and option derivation. Acceptance: centralize board-index lookup by :index for app-state subscriptions, move-selection, and UI labels; add coverage for selecting/targeting a surviving territory after a territory is destroyed and after creating a new territory in a gapped board.
close_reason: Fixed app-state and move-selection board lookups to use stable cell indexes with gapped-board coverage

title: Render pyramid piece edges with black outlines
description:
Goal: make Icehouse pyramid pieces easier to read on tarot cards by rendering visible black edge lines around each pyramid.

Requirements:
- Add a visual edge treatment to 3D pyramid meshes so their silhouettes and side-face boundaries remain visible against card art.
- Cover upright pieces and cardinal/lying pieces without changing existing piece position, orientation, or selection behavior.
- Prefer a Three.js-native approach such as edge geometry/line segments or an equivalent overlay that works with the current CDN r128 global build.
- Keep the CSS fallback behavior intact; add a matching fallback outline only if needed for visual consistency.
- Update externs if new Three.js APIs are used by advanced compilation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pyramids render with visible black edge lines in the 3D board view.
- Existing piece orientation and height tests still pass, and release compilation succeeds.
- A smoke or focused rendering check covers at least one selected viewport where edge outlines are expected to be present.
close_reason: done

title: Fail feature tests when parsed scenarios or steps are empty
description: The gameplay feature test only asserts that at least one .feature file exists, then calls assert-results-pass for each parsed result. feature-runner/assert-results-pass performs one assertion per result but returns true for an empty result collection, and run-scenario returns success for a scenario with no steps. A parser regression, empty feature file, misplaced Scenario heading, or accidentally blank scenario could therefore be reported as a passing test run with no scenario assertions for that file. Acceptance: gameplay feature tests assert each feature file expands to at least one scenario, and either the parser or runner rejects scenarios with zero executable steps; add regression coverage for empty feature files and empty scenarios so this cannot become a vacuous pass.

title: Make World and Fool command-schema tests validate delegated command shapes
description: The representative command-schema tests for World and Fool can pass without proving the delegated command fields are meaningful. WorldCommand only requires :copied-board-index and Malli map schemas allow unrelated extra keys, so the current test World :target/:orientation data is accepted because it is ignored; invalid delegated data like :target {:kind :nonsense} still validates. FoolReveal accepts :command and :play-command as opaque :map, so a reveal play command with nonsense target shape also validates. This makes those tests partly vacuous for the high-variance command contracts they are meant to cover. Acceptance: model World delegated copied-power command fields and Fool revealed play-command shapes by reusing the appropriate child schemas or add explicit documented boundaries where semantic validation is deferred; add negative tests showing malformed delegated World/Fool commands fail the command contract when they are supposed to be machine-checkable.

title: Enforce Cup target range for territory and enemy-piece creation
description:
Fresh review found Cup targeting is split. src/main/gnostica/game_state/cup.cljc checks cup-targetable-coordinate? for wasteland territory creation, but place-small-piece and create-enemy-small-piece do not check the acting minion target coordinate. Browser staging mirrors the gap: cup-target-piece? only checks enemy/territory shape and move-target-board-options falls through to every board cell for Cup and Sun Cup. A hand-card Cup through a minion on board index 0 facing east can create a small piece or enemy small at board index 8. This violates docs/rules.txt target-space rules for minions and Cups.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pure Cup rejects out-of-range territory and enemy-piece targets with structured errors.
- Browser options/filtering use the same target-coordinate rule for plain Cup, Sun Cup, Empress/Temperance/Lovers Cup actions, and World-copied Cup.
- Tests cover out-of-range pure commands and browser staging so illegal distant targets cannot reach confirm.
close_reason: Enforced Cup target-space validation in pure gameplay and browser staging, with regression coverage.

title: Mind map node [5] references a missing linked gist
description: MIND_MAP.md node [5] says the methodology includes a bootstrap prompt as a linked gist, but the node and surrounding file do not contain a gist URL or any actual link. This is small, but it weakens the self-documentation instructions because agents are told the reference exists when it does not. Evidence: MIND_MAP.md line 15. Add the link if it is still useful, or remove the phrase linked gist from the preserved generic node if that reference should not be part of this repository copy.
close_reason: Removed unsupported linked gist reference from MIND_MAP.md node [5].

title: Clarify card-zone docs now that Cup hand cards discard
description: README.md:41 and AGENTS.md:27 still imply drawing and discarding are entirely future pure gameplay transitions. But gnostica.game-state/apply-cup-move already removes a hand-source Cup card and app-state card-zone tests assert the discard pile updates. Clarify that the missing transition is the standalone draw/discard turn option, not every discard mutation, and keep README, AGENTS, and MIND_MAP wording aligned.
close_reason: Clarified card-zone docs for Cup hand-card discard behavior

title: pressing the I hotkey should not reset the view
close_reason: done

title: allow wasd style hotkey and arrow key movement of the camera/world
close_reason: done

title: Replace raw app-db subscriptions with composed view-model subscriptions
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: app.cljs registers most subscriptions as direct app-db query functions at lines 139-302, then views compose many raw subscriptions themselves. board-stage derives pieces-by-space, wastelands, and bounds in the view at lines 455-459. move-active-controls dereferences roughly sixteen subscriptions at lines 907-924 before deciding which controls to render. This blurs the subscription layer: derived data and view models are being assembled in Reagent render functions instead of a composed signal graph.

Acceptance criteria:
- Split subscriptions into extractor subscriptions and composed view-model subscriptions for board, card zones, move panel, and dialogs.
- Move board layout derivations and move-control option aggregation out of views and behind subscriptions where they are cached/reactive.
- Reduce high-level views to one or two feature-level subscriptions plus event dispatch callbacks.
- Preserve existing DOM output and app-state behavior.
close_reason: Implemented composed view-model subscriptions for board, card zones, move panel, dialogs, header, and app shell.

title: Represent Rod unbounded variants separately from plain Rod
description: src/main/gnostica/cards.cljc treats :rod-unbounded and :wild-suits as plain Rod sources. The icon guide documents unbounded Rod behavior as ignoring normal space-limit blockers, while resolve/apply-rod-command always enforce the ordinary full-destination check and has no variant field. Acceptance: carry the Rod variant into commands and implement the unbounded behavior or explicitly reject/document it until supported; add tests for full-space behavior.
close_reason: done

title: Make main board view fill the browser window
description: The primary game and board view should take the full available browser window in normal play. Secondary panels such as move controls, territory details, card zones, and help should open as overlays that hover above the main view and dock to the sides as needed instead of permanently reducing the main view's usable space. Acceptance criteria: the main board/game view fills the viewport; side panels can be opened and closed and render above the main view; panels avoid blocking core board interactions where practical and remain usable on desktop and mobile; existing Three.js and CSS fallback layouts continue to work.
close_reason: done

title: No-piece players can end their turn without initial placement
description: docs/rules.txt says that if a player has no pieces on the board, they may only put a small piece in any orientation on an empty territory or wasteland. The public end-turn path in src/main/gnostica/game_state/core.cljc does not enforce this no-piece turn restriction, and the header enables End turn whenever there is a current unfinished player. Repro from a fresh game with no board pieces: (gnostica.game-state/end-turn state {:player-id :rose}) succeeds and advances to Indigo. Require initial placement before a no-piece player can end the turn, or explicitly document and test an intentional pass rule if this behavior is deliberate.
close_reason: Require initial placement before end turn

title: Document br issue tracking outside AGENTS
description:
AGENTS.md documents the br/beads_rust issue tracker, including br sync --flush-only and committing .beads changes with Jujutsu. README.md and MIND_MAP.md contain no issue-tracking guidance, even though tickets are repo artifacts under .beads and the mind map is the primary knowledge index for agents. Contributors or agents starting from README/MIND_MAP can miss the expected tracker and label workflow.

Acceptance: add concise issue-tracking guidance to README.md and a MIND_MAP node or link, keep AGENTS.md as the detailed source, mention br sync --flush-only plus .beads/Jujutsu handling, and avoid duplicating long CLI help.
close_reason: done

title: Guard casual lobby start while bidding is staged
description: The lobby UI hides Start with first seat while [:lobby :starting-bid] is active, but app-state/start-lobby-game and the :gnostica.app/start-lobby-game event still allow a programmatic or stale dispatch to create top-level :game before confirm-lobby-bidding. That violates the bidding flow guarantee that bid card selection does not mutate :game until the start decision is confirmed. Acceptance: start-lobby-game rejects or ignores attempts while starting-bid is active, preserves nil top-level :game, reports a structured lobby error or unchanged db consistently, and has focused app-state/handler tests.
close_reason: Fixed by guarding start-lobby-game while starting bids are active

title: Implement or hide unavailable orient and initial-placement flows
description: README.md:55 and AGENTS.md:37 describe the move-selection flow, but src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc exposes :orient-piece and :place-initial-small as selectable sources that can reach confirm and then fall through to :move-transition-unavailable. Initial small placement also stages only existing territories, while docs/rules.txt allows an empty territory or wasteland when a player has no pieces. Acceptance: implement pure transitions and UI targets, or keep these sources disabled/clearly unavailable until implemented; cover successful and rejected confirmations.
close_reason: Implemented orient and initial placement transitions

title: Fool reveal play cannot use full major-arcana powers
description: docs/rules.txt says Fool turns over the next draw-pile card and plays it, with using that revealed card's powers optional. The pure Fool implementation in src/main/gnostica/game_state/draw.cljc only routes revealed play commands through #{:cup :rod :disc :sword}. That lets a revealed major use a plain suit subset, but cannot play full implemented major powers such as Justice hand-trade plus Sword, Death double Sword, Hanged Man Rod plus hand trade, Devil orientations, draw majors, or World delegation. Expand Fool revealed-card routing to implemented major facades, keep the revealed card already in discard so it is not charged twice, and cover at least one revealed composite/major-power regression.
close_reason: Fixed: Fool revealed-card play now routes implemented suit and full major powers without double-charging revealed sources.

title: Define Swords command contract and card helpers
description: Introduce the browser-free command/action shape and reusable legality helpers for Swords. Acceptance: commands explicitly identify player id, source as territory or hand card, acting minion piece id, target as piece or territory, selected sword variant, damage amount, optional replacement-card source/id for territory attacks, and optional orientation when shrinking one of the current player's pieces. Card helpers identify Swords minor cards plus Sword-bearing majors such as Magician, Justice, Death, Tower, and Moon; target resolution follows docs/rules.txt targeting rules for upright/current-space, cardinal adjacent-space, and minion self-targeting; invalid commands return structured errors without mutating game state.
close_reason: Implemented Sword card helpers and browser-free resolve-sword-command contract with focused tests and docs

title: Unimplemented Sword-only sources are staged as Cup instead of unavailable
description: README.md says move sources without a pure transition return an explicit move-transition-unavailable result, and AGENTS.md asks for explicit unavailable results rather than silent mutation. Current move selection does not model Sword as an implemented power, but selected-power returns :cup when the selected source card has no Cup/Rod/Disc options. A Swords-only source therefore walks the user through Cup target controls and fails only after confirmation with source-card-not-cup. app_state_test currently codifies that behavior in rejected-cup-confirmation-keeps-staged-selection. Evidence: README.md line 61, AGENTS.md lines 33 and 47, src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc selected-power and move-power-order, test/gnostica/app_state_test.clj around the rejected Cup confirmation. Decide whether to expose Sword as unavailable, return move-transition-unavailable immediately, or implement Sword confirmation wiring, then update the tests and docs.
close_reason: Fixed Sword-only move sources to stage as Sword and confirm unavailable

title: Move pyramid pips to side faces near the base edge
description:
Goal: adjust Icehouse pyramid pip marker placement so pips sit on the side faces, next to the edge by the base of each pyramid.

Requirements:
- Move 3D pip markers from the current base-facing placement onto pyramid side faces near the base edge.
- Keep one, two, and three pip counts visually clear for small, medium, and large pieces.
- Preserve black piece edge outlines, piece color contrast, board/card readability, and existing piece orientation behavior.
- Update browser-free layout helpers and tests if the placement math remains pure.
- Keep the change visual-only: do not alter piece ownership, board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Upright and lying/cardinal pyramids show their pips along side faces next to the base edge.
- Pip counts remain legible at the default desktop and mobile board views.
- Existing tests, release compilation, and the 3D smoke check pass.
- A focused layout or smoke assertion covers the corrected pip placement if practical.
close_reason: done

title: Clear stale lobby bid errors when bid choice is cleared
description: select-lobby-bid-card clears a player bid when the card id is blank, but that branch only dissocs [:lobby :starting-bid :current-bids] and does not clear [:lobby :error]. If an invalid or incomplete bid error is present, clearing or resetting a bid can leave a stale alert until another valid selection or reveal action. Acceptance: clearing a bid choice removes stale bid-selection errors while preserving unrelated validation errors, and app-state tests cover invalid selection then clear and incomplete reveal then changed selection.
close_reason: Fixed stale lobby starting-bid error clearing

title: Move demo and smoke fixtures out of production app flow
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: demo and verification fixtures are part of production namespaces. src/main/gnostica/pieces.cljc defines initial-pieces, app_state.cljc injects default demo board pieces during normal initialization, and app.cljs has the smoke-only URL/deck-order hook around lines 1235-1259. That couples domain models and browser startup to demo/smoke setup concerns.

Acceptance criteria:
- Introduce a fixture/demo namespace for seeded visible pieces and smoke deck orders.
- Make production initialization opt into fixtures through explicit init options instead of hard-coded defaults in core model/app namespaces.
- Keep default local demo behavior if desired, but make the production model and browser startup boundaries free of smoke-specific logic.
- Update smoke/app-state tests to use the fixture namespace directly.
close_reason: Implemented demo/smoke fixture extraction

title: Reconcile demo board pieces with stash accounting
description: README.md:41 and MIND_MAP.md:35 say demo pieces are kept in the authoritative :game [:pieces :on-board]. src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc injects pieces/initial-pieces by associng :pieces :on-board, but player :stash values and [:pieces :stashes] remain full. Cup creation reads those stash counts, so the default app state can create extra small pieces and current schema/tests do not catch the mismatch. Acceptance: either remove demo pieces from authoritative gameplay setup, decrement both stash mirrors when seeding them, or make them renderer-only; add coverage for stash/piece count consistency.
close_reason: done

title: Wheel draw-pile territory creation does not reshuffle exhausted draw pile
description: docs/rules.txt says the discard pile is reshuffled whenever the draw pile is exhausted. Wheel of Fortune can create a territory from the first card of the draw pile, but src/main/gnostica/game_state/cup.cljc simply rejects :draw-pile-top territory creation when :draw-pile is empty. Repro: give Rose Wheel in hand, put the minion at board index 3 facing west, set :draw-pile [] and :discard-pile [World], then apply Cup with :territory-card-source :draw-pile-top to wasteland row 1 col -1. The result is :draw-pile-empty instead of reshuffling discard and creating the territory. Add a deterministic :shuffle-fn boundary for this transition, preserve source-card/deck accounting, and test the empty-draw-pile path.
close_reason: Fixed Wheel draw-pile territory creation to reshuffle discard pile with deterministic :shuffle-fn; added tests.

title: Harden extracted move-selection helper contexts
description: The current move-selection split keeps controls.cljc, targets.cljc, and commands.cljc browser-free by passing large ad hoc context maps from src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc. Each helper namespace defines a local call/value wrapper around (get ctx key), so missing or renamed dependencies fail later as generic runtime exceptions, and there is no small contract that says which keys each extracted namespace needs. Evidence: control-context around move_selection.cljc:1972, target-context around :5287, command-context around :5329, plus call/value wrappers in move_selection/controls.cljc, targets.cljc, and commands.cljc. Acceptance: replace the opaque context maps with explicit smaller dependency records/constructors/specs or direct cohesive helper extraction; add tests or assertions that fail clearly when a required dependency is absent; keep the public gnostica.move-selection facade stable.
close_reason: Added explicit move-selection helper context constructors with required dependency checks and focused tests.

title: Render first-piece drag ghost under pointer
description:
When the current player drags the small stash-piece source from the Move panel to place their first piece, the UI should render a piece-shaped drag ghost under the pointer so the user understands what object is being dragged.

Context:
- Source is the Place first piece option in the Move panel.
- Applies to the normal Three.js board path and should not regress the CSS fallback.
- The ghost should use the current player's color and small-piece shape, follow the pointer during the drag, and disappear on drop/cancel.
- It should work before orientation is chosen; orientation can remain neutral/upright until the user selects a direction.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dragging the first-piece source visibly shows a small current-player piece under or near the pointer.
- Dropping still stages initial placement without mutating :game before confirmation.
- Disabled direct manipulation / Detailed entry fallback remains usable.
- Smoke or browser-facing coverage verifies the drag affordance is present for the first-piece path.
close_reason: Rendered first-piece drag ghost under pointer with smoke coverage

title: Clean up or configure clojure-lsp diagnostics warnings
description: Fresh diagnostics run completed tests/release successfully but clojure-lsp diagnostics exits nonzero because of warnings. Actionable examples include unused bindings in src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc:978 and src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc:230, unused private vars such as move_selection/discard-card-by-id and manipulation/source-card-id, an unused clojure.string require in fixtures.cljc, plus a redundant let in test/gnostica/smoke/browser.clj. Many unused-public-var infos are intentional facade/export shims and may need configuration rather than deletion. Acceptance: remove genuine dead bindings/vars/requires, configure intentional public facade exports, and make clojure-lsp diagnostics useful as a clean verification signal.
close_reason: Removed real clojure-lsp warnings and configured intentional unused-public-var infos off.

title: Lower lying-down pieces onto card surface
description: Lying-down Icehouse pieces in the Three.js board renderer appear to float slightly above the card surface. Adjust the side/cardinal orientation piece height/placement so those pieces rest directly on the card while preserving upright piece placement, pip markers, edge outlines, and existing board-layout smoke checks.
close_reason: Lowered cardinal piece contact height onto card surface

title: Display player hand, draw deck, and discard pile
description:
Goal: show the current player's hand of cards, the deck they draw from, and the discard pile in the browser UI.

Requirements:
- Derive hand, draw pile, and discard pile from the authoritative game state under app-db :game; do not duplicate card-zone state at the top level.
- Display the current player's six-card hand with card titles and images where practical, preserving responsive layout and readable card art.
- Display the draw deck as a distinct zone with at least remaining-card count and a clear facedown/deck affordance.
- Display the discard pile as a distinct zone with count and latest/top discarded card when present, plus an empty state when absent.
- Keep this UI-only unless pure gameplay transitions already exist; do not silently mutate :game for drawing or discarding.
- Ensure the card zones fit alongside the existing board, move panel, and territory panel on desktop and mobile without overlap.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the app layout, subscriptions, or verification workflow changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- The current player's hand, draw deck, and discard pile are visible from the main game screen.
- Zone contents are derived from gnostica.game-state data and update when app-db :game changes.
- Empty and non-empty discard pile states render clearly.
- clojure -M:test passes, and release/smoke checks are updated or run if the visible layout changes.
close_reason: Implemented current-player hand, draw deck, and discard pile UI

title: Extract Three.js texture and icon canvas rendering
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: src/main/gnostica/three_board.cljs mixes scene lifecycle with a local Canvas icon renderer. It has icon drawing primitives and a 130-line draw-icon-mark! around lines 238-367 that parallels gnostica.icon-view.cljs, plus card image cover and canvas texture composition around lines 399-475. The board namespace now owns both visual asset generation and Three.js orchestration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move card texture composition and Canvas icon drawing into a dedicated namespace, for example gnostica.three-card-textures or gnostica.icon-texture.
- Keep the Three.js board responsible for mesh/material hookup, not symbol drawing details.
- Add focused tests or smoke-visible metadata to guard icon count/layout parity with icon_view where practical.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Make board base colour dark velvet
description:
Goal: adjust the board base/table colour so the play surface reads as dark velvet.

Requirements:
- Update the 3D board base/table material colour to a dark velvet tone.
- Keep tarot card art, piece colours, pip markers, black outlines, and wasteland outlines readable against the darker base.
- Keep the CSS fallback board surface visually consistent with the 3D base colour.
- Update smoke/layout assertions or documented expected colour metadata if they currently pin the old base colour.
- Keep this visual-only: do not alter board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Desktop and mobile 3D board views show a dark velvet base behind the cards and pieces.
- CSS fallback paths use a matching dark velvet base.
- Existing tests, release compilation, and 3D smoke checks pass.
close_reason: Implemented dark velvet 3D and fallback board base color with updated smoke expectations

title: Add pip/notch size markers to pyramid pieces
description:
Goal: add small visual size markers near the base of every rendered Icehouse/Looney pyramid so players can identify size at a glance without relying only on mesh height.

Background: Looney pyramid pieces use pips as their size convention. Looney Labs says each piece is marked with 1, 2, or 3 pips, and the Pyramid Arcade guide defines pips as teardrop-shaped marks: smalls have one pip, mediums two pips, larges three pips. Wired similarly notes small pips near the base in counts of 1, 2, and 3.

Requirements:
- Add visible notch/pip-style marks near the base of pyramid pieces: one for small, two for medium, three for large.
- Apply the markers to the primary Three.js pyramid meshes and keep them visible enough from the default board camera.
- Update the CSS fallback piece rendering with matching size markers.
- Keep markers aligned with piece color/lighting/orientation and avoid interfering with selection raycasts or board/card picking.
- Prefer a Looney-pyramid-inspired mark, but avoid copying exact proprietary piece geometry beyond the general one/two/three pip convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Small, medium, and large pieces are visually distinguishable by marker count in both 3D and CSS fallback views.
- Upright and cardinal/lying pieces still render with correct orientation and board placement.
- Existing piece tests remain green, and new focused tests or smoke notes cover marker count mapping if practical.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if renderer behavior or verification guidance changes.

Reference details:
- https://www.looneylabs.com/pyramids-equipment
- https://www.looneylabs.com/sites/default/files/literature/Pyramids_Instruction_Guide.pdf
- https://www.wired.com/2013/01/looney-pyramids/
close_reason: Implemented 3D and CSS pip markers with tests and docs

title: background velvet to be darker
close_reason: done

title: Refresh MIND_MAP rules-source anchor beyond board setup
description: MIND_MAP.md node [7] still frames board setup as the implemented rule slice, but the current code and later nodes also cover six-card hands, draw/discard zones, turn metadata, and Cup move transitions. As an anchor node, this can send agents into an outdated mental model. Update [7] to summarize the current implemented rule slices and link to the game-state/Cup nodes.
close_reason: Updated MIND_MAP rules-source anchor to cover current implemented setup, card-zone, turn, and Cup rule slices.

title: Make MIND_MAP opening guidance point agents at Gnostica nodes
description: MIND_MAP.md:3 tells agents to read overview nodes 1-5 first and treats them as the primary knowledge index, but nodes 1-5 are generic mind-map format documentation while the actual Gnostica overview starts at node 6. Adjust the guidance or add project overview anchors so agents do not spend the first pass on format mechanics instead of repo facts. Preserve any intentional format rules explicitly.
close_reason: done

title: Epic: Direct manipulation move entry
description:
Goal: make move entry feel like manipulating the tabletop directly: drag cards, pieces, territories, draw pile, discard pile, and stash pieces to express legal Gnostica moves.

Keep the current staged move panel approach available as an alternative option. The redesign should make direct manipulation the primary path, but the existing declarative control-groups flow should remain reachable for accessibility, precision, debugging, and complex edge cases.

Architecture constraints:
- Do not change the browser-free game-state facades as the source of truth.
- Direct gestures should build the same command maps currently confirmed through gnostica.move-selection and gnostica.app-state.
- Game state must change only after explicit confirmation or an equivalent user-approved commit action.
- Preserve stable board cell indexes and wasteland coordinates; do not treat board vector positions as stable ids.
- Preserve deterministic shuffle injection for draw-major and Wheel flows.

Acceptance criteria:
- A set of implementation tickets exists for gesture state, legal-target highlighting, card dragging, piece dragging, replacement-card gestures, major sequencing, 3D/CSS pointer support, fallback accessibility, and verification.
- Each child ticket states how the current staged move panel remains available.
- The final feature can be adopted incrementally without blocking current move-entry behavior.
close_reason: All direct manipulation child tickets are closed; gesture staging, fallback detailed entry, browser pointer support, action ribbons, and verification coverage are implemented and documented.

title: Model gesture intents and pending move confirmation
description:
Introduce a gesture-intent layer above the existing move-selection state machine.

Problem:
The current move panel asks players to choose source, power, minion, target, replacement, damage, distance, and orientation through form controls. Direct manipulation needs an intermediate state that can represent a drag or tap gesture, infer candidate commands, show missing choices, and still confirm through the existing pure transitions.

Scope:
- Define a browser-free data shape for active gesture, inferred source, target, legal alternatives, preview command, missing required fields, and structured errors.
- Keep the staged selection under top-level :move-selection and do not duplicate authoritative game data outside :game.
- Add app-state facade functions/events that translate gesture intents into the same staged command data used by current confirmations.
- Add a compact pending-move tray view model that can show inferred command summary, missing choices, error state, Confirm, Cancel, and a switch to detailed mode.
- Preserve explicit confirmation before mutating :game.

Fallback requirement:
The existing staged move panel remains available from the pending tray as Detailed entry. It must be able to continue or replace a gesture-started move without corrupting selection state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests cover gesture intent initialization, cancellation, conversion to existing staged fields, and rejection without game mutation.
- Existing move-panel tests still pass.
- No game-state facade signatures change.
close_reason: Implemented browser-free gesture intent staging, pending tray view model, app events, and tests.

title: Write the direct move-entry interaction spec
description:
Create a concrete interaction spec before broad implementation so direct manipulation stays coherent across cards, pieces, territories, and major-power sequences.

Scope:
- Document the object grammar: what happens when players drag or tap hand cards, territory cards, pieces, stash pieces, draw deck, and discard pile cards.
- Define visual states for idle, draggable, armed source, legal target, disabled target, preview, missing choice, error, pending confirmation, and consumed-turn disabled.
- Define ambiguity handling when a gesture could mean multiple powers, variants, target kinds, action counts, or replacement sources.
- Define mobile/touch behavior, click/tap alternatives, keyboard alternatives, and reduced-motion behavior.
- Map representative gestures to the existing pure command contracts for Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, Sun, major sequences, draw majors, manipulation majors, World, orient, and initial placement.
- Identify implementation phases that allow the current staged move panel to remain the alternative option throughout the rollout.

Acceptance criteria:
- The spec is checked into docs or another agreed project location.
- It includes at least one end-to-end example for each suit family plus one ordered major, one draw major, World, orient, and initial placement.
- It names the fallback entry point for Detailed or Classic controls and explains when the UI should recommend it.
- It avoids changing rules behavior; all examples point back to existing game-state facades.
close_reason: Added docs/direct_move_entry_spec.md with direct move-entry grammar, fallback path, examples, and rollout phases.

title: Derive legal targets and highlights for active move intents
description:
Add shared legal-target affordance data so the board, hand, discard pile, draw pile, and stash can show what a player can do before they drop or confirm.

Problem:
Direct manipulation only feels natural if legal targets light up as soon as the player picks up or selects a card, piece, territory, or stash piece. Invalid targets should remain understandable, not silently fail.

Scope:
- Extend move-panel or app-state view models with legal target descriptors for territories, wastelands, pieces, hand cards, discard cards, draw deck, and stash pieces.
- Reuse existing rule-heavy option derivation and pure resolvers for Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, Sun, World, manipulation majors, orient, initial placement, draw, and draw-major flows.
- Include reason data for disabled targets where practical, using existing structured error codes/messages.
- Keep stable board cell :index values and explicit wasteland coordinate keys in all target descriptors.
- Render highlights in both Three.js and CSS fallback without changing game state.

Fallback requirement:
The current staged move panel should consume the same legal target descriptors for option lists where possible, so the detailed entry path gets clearer validation without depending on drag support.

Acceptance criteria:
- Starting a source selection exposes legal targets for at least current implemented Cup/Rod/Disc/Sword/orient/initial-placement flows.
- Invalid or unavailable targets are visibly distinct from legal targets and can surface a concise reason.
- Tests cover destroyed-territory gaps and appended territory indexes.
- No direct DOM, Three.js, or re-frame global reads are added to pure option derivation.
close_reason: Implemented shared legal-target descriptors and board/card-zone/move-panel highlights.

title: Support direct card gestures from hand, territories, draw pile, and discard pile
description:
Let players express card-powered moves by moving actual cards instead of first filling source and power controls.

Scope:
- Drag or tap-hold a hand card to arm it as a source and show legal board, piece, wasteland, draw, discard, and replacement targets.
- Allow territory source activation by selecting or dragging a board territory card, with minion scope inferred from owned pieces touching that territory.
- Support standalone draw/discard interaction by dragging hand cards to discard and using the draw deck affordance for draw count where possible.
- Support Judgement-style discard selection and Fool/High Priestess draw-major staging as direct interactions with the draw and discard zones.
- When a card exposes multiple implemented powers, show a small contextual power chooser near the card or drop target instead of sending the player to the full panel first.
- Keep hand-source card cost and discard behavior delegated to existing pure transitions.

Fallback requirement:
Every direct card gesture must have an equivalent path in the staged move panel. A player can switch from a gesture-started card move into Detailed entry and finish it through the current controls.

Acceptance criteria:
- A hand Cup card can be dragged to a legal territory or wasteland and produce the same pending command that the current panel would confirm.
- A territory source can be selected directly from the board and used without duplicating board state.
- Multi-power cards surface only implemented powers plus explicit unavailable options where applicable.
- Rejected drops keep the staged selection and show structured error feedback without mutating :game.
close_reason: Implemented direct card gesture entry with pending tray and CSS fallback drops

title: Support direct piece, stash, and territory movement gestures
description:
Let players move and orient physical game objects directly on the board.

Scope:
- Drag the current player small stash piece to an empty territory or legal wasteland for initial placement.
- Drag owned pieces to express orient-only moves, Rod minion movement, Hermit piece relocation, and composite-major movement steps when an appropriate source is active.
- Drag target pieces in the minion direction to express Rod push-piece commands, with snapped cardinal paths and distance preview.
- Drag eligible territory cards to legal wastelands for Rod push-territory or Hermit territory relocation, preserving the rules that source-coordinate pieces remain behind where required.
- Provide a compass or ring control for orientation whenever the target command allows current-player orientation.
- Preserve enemy piece orientation automatically where rules require it.

Fallback requirement:
The existing staged controls for source, minion, Rod mode, target piece, distance, destination, and orientation remain available. Gesture previews should populate those controls when switching to Detailed entry.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initial placement can be completed by dragging a stash piece to a legal empty target and choosing orientation.
- A Rod move previews path, distance, destination, full-territory rejection, and void rejection before confirmation.
- Hermit piece and territory relocation gestures build the same commands as the current manipulation-major staging.
- Tests cover own versus enemy orientation behavior and no mutation on invalid drops.
close_reason: Implemented direct stash, piece, Rod, and Hermit movement gesture staging with CSS fallback affordances, pending previews, and tests.

title: Support replacement-card, growth, shrink, and damage gestures
description:
Make Disc and Sword style mutations feel like changing the object on the table.

Scope:
- Drag a Disc source onto a piece to grow it, showing whether the target owner has the needed larger stash piece.
- Drag a Sword source onto a piece to shrink or destroy it, with a compact damage selector constrained by target pips and source rules.
- Drag valid replacement cards from hand or allowed discard-pile sources onto territories for Disc growth, Sword shrink, Star, Tower, and Sun replacement flows.
- Highlight only replacement cards with the exact required point value, while showing why invalid cards are unavailable.
- For same-target shortcuts such as Strength, Death, and Sun, allow the gesture sequence to represent the shortcut while still making the skipped intermediate resource explicit in the preview.
- Preserve card accounting by delegating source charging and replacement movement to pure transitions.

Fallback requirement:
The current detailed controls for target kind, replacement source/card, damage, action count, and orientation stay available. Dragging a replacement card should fill the corresponding staged fields when the user switches to Detailed entry.

Acceptance criteria:
- Disc territory growth can be staged by dragging a valid +1 replacement card onto the target territory.
- Sword territory shrink can be staged by choosing damage and dragging a valid lower-value replacement card onto the territory.
- Star and Tower discard-pile replacement options work only when the active source permits them.
- Invalid replacements produce structured error or disabled-reason feedback and do not mutate :game.
close_reason: Implemented source-aware Disc/Sword replacement-card gestures

title: Add an action ribbon for ordered major powers and World copies
description:
Replace complex major-power form staging with a visible sequence of tabletop actions.

Problem:
Composite majors, Sword-related majors, Sun, World, Devil, Fool, High Priestess, and Judgement often require ordered optional steps. Direct manipulation needs a clear way to show what has happened, what can happen next, and when the player can stop.

Scope:
- Add a compact action ribbon that displays completed sub-actions, active required or optional step, skipped steps, and Confirm/Cancel controls.
- Route each ribbon step through the existing apply-major-sequence based command contracts where applicable.
- Support Empress, Emperor, Lovers, Chariot, Hanged Man, Temperance, Justice, Death, Tower, Moon, Sun, Devil, Fool, High Priestess, Judgement, and World copy flows at the same implementation level as current browser staging.
- Let World choose a copyable board major directly, then reuse the copied power interaction model while preserving World as the paid source.
- Allow same-target shortcuts to appear as one clear compound step rather than hidden form state.

Fallback requirement:
The existing staged major controls remain available as Detailed entry for every ribbon flow. The ribbon must be able to show steps created through either direct gestures or the current controls.

Acceptance criteria:
- A player can complete at least one ordered composite major through direct board gestures and the ribbon.
- Hanged Man and Justice trade-only paths are represented without forcing an unrelated target step.
- World copy selection uses stable board indexes and rejects minor or World copy targets.
- Existing browser staging tests continue to pass or are adapted without losing fallback coverage.
close_reason: Implemented compact action ribbon for ordered major, World, Sun, draw-major, and Devil flows while preserving detailed move controls; tests, release, and smoke pass.

title: Implement shared pointer infrastructure for 3D and CSS drag previews
description:
Build the renderer-level event support needed for drag-based move entry across the Three.js board and CSS fallback.

Scope:
- Extend Three.js picking beyond territory card selection to identify draggable card planes, piece meshes, wasteland outlines, and drop targets using stable game object ids.
- Suppress OrbitControls only while a real game-object drag is active; empty-table gestures should continue to orbit, zoom, or pan normally.
- Render ghost previews, snapped movement paths, target outlines, invalid-drop feedback, and pending-command status without mutating :game.
- Provide equivalent CSS fallback pointer behavior for board cards, wastelands, pieces, hand cards, discard cards, draw deck, and stash pieces.
- Keep renderer-specific DOM/WebGL details out of browser-free game-state and option-derivation namespaces.
- Handle mouse, touch, and pen input consistently through pointer events where possible.

Fallback requirement:
If pointer dragging is unavailable or disabled, the staged move panel must remain fully usable. The renderer should expose a preference or capability flag that can force Detailed entry.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dragging a card or piece on the 3D board no longer triggers accidental card inspection or OrbitControls movement.
- The CSS fallback exposes matching drop-target behavior for the same object categories.
- Smoke or integration tests verify nonblank board rendering still works with drag affordances enabled.
- Renderer cleanup removes drag listeners and temporary meshes on unmount.
close_reason: Implemented shared gesture payload helpers, Three.js object drag/drop picking for card planes, pieces, and wasteland targets, renderer target feedback, detailed-entry capability flag, and verification.

title: Preserve staged move panel as an accessible alternative input mode
description:
Keep the current explicit staged move-entry approach available after direct manipulation becomes the primary interaction.

Scope:
- Add a clear Detailed entry or Classic controls affordance from the pending move tray, header, or move surface.
- Preserve keyboard and non-drag workflows for every implemented move path.
- Ensure screen-reader friendly controls exist for source, power, minion, target, replacement, distance, damage, orientation, draw count, major action count, and confirmation.
- Allow users to start with a drag gesture and finish in Detailed entry, or start in Detailed entry and use board clicks or drag highlights for target selection.
- Keep explicit unavailable results for powers without browser confirmation wiring.
- Add a user setting or session toggle that can make Detailed entry the default.

Acceptance criteria:
- All currently implemented move-panel flows remain reachable without drag gestures.
- A user can switch between direct and detailed modes mid-selection without losing valid staged data.
- Keyboard-only source/target selection remains possible for representative Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, orient, initial placement, and major flows.
- Accessibility tests or documented manual checks cover focus order, visible focus, and non-pointer confirmation.
close_reason: Added Detailed entry default setting, preserved staged data through gesture handoff, disabled pointer drags without removing classic controls, documented accessibility fallback checks, and verified with test/release/smoke.

title: Add verification for direct manipulation and fallback move entry
description:
Add test and smoke coverage that proves direct manipulation builds the same game commands as the staged move panel and that fallback mode remains intact.

Scope:
- Unit-test browser-free gesture intent, legal target derivation, command construction, cancellation, and structured rejection preservation.
- Add app-state tests for gesture-to-command conversion, switch-to-detailed behavior, consumed-turn disabling, stable board-index targeting, and no mutation on rejected pending moves.
- Add representative browser or smoke coverage for 3D drag gestures, CSS fallback gestures, pending tray rendering, and Detailed entry fallback.
- Reuse existing pure game-state tests for rule correctness instead of duplicating every gameplay rule at the UI layer.
- Include mobile/touch viewport coverage for at least one card drag and one piece drag path.
- Confirm direct gestures and current staged controls produce equivalent command maps for selected Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, orient, initial-placement, and major-power examples.

Fallback requirement:
Verification must include a run where direct dragging is disabled or unavailable and the current staged move panel still completes representative moves.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests fail if a direct gesture mutates :game before confirmation.
- Tests fail if detailed move entry disappears or can no longer complete existing implemented flows.
- Smoke coverage still checks Three.js readiness, CSS fallback, card zones, help dialogs, and board selection behavior after drag support is added.
- Documentation updates identify the verification commands needed for this feature.
close_reason: Added direct manipulation command-equivalence tests, disabled-drag Detailed entry fallback coverage, browser smoke probes for Three.js/card/CSS gestures, and verification docs.

title: Fix MIND_MAP navigation wording around format nodes and project anchors
description: MIND_MAP.md:3 tells agents to start at project anchors [6-10] and consult [1-5] only for format rules, but node [5] says overview nodes [1-5] serve as navigation hubs. That generic format text conflicts with this file's project navigation model and could send agents back into format nodes instead of project anchors. Acceptance: reconcile the wording without breaking the first-five-node convention, or add a project-specific clarification outside immutable format nodes.
close_reason: done

title: Browser/re-frame coverage misses composed move-panel subscription regressions
description: clojure -M:test exercises app-state/move-panel-view directly and clojure -M:smoke exercises board, overlays, hotkeys, and selection, but neither path validates the registered re-frame :gnostica.app/move-panel-view subscription shape or renders a move-panel flow through the browser. That allowed the subscription to omit :control-groups while tests and smoke still passed. Acceptance: add focused coverage that drives the registered re-frame event/subscription path or a smoke move-panel interaction and asserts active control groups render for at least one staged move.
close_reason: Fixed move-panel control groups subscription and added browser smoke coverage for staged hand-card controls

title: Increase special move icon size
description: Increase the rendered size of the special move/Gnostica card icons, using an initial target of 100% larger than the current size. Acceptance: DOM card overlays and Three.js generated card textures both use the larger icon scale; one-, two-, and three-icon cards remain legible without clipping or incoherent overlap; the size change is centralized where practical rather than duplicated per renderer; tests or smoke coverage are updated where existing icon rendering checks depend on sizing.
close_reason: done

title: Recompress MIND_MAP after direct manipulation expansion
description: MIND_MAP.md now has 52 numbered nodes even though line 3 says to keep it compact at 20-50 nodes typical, and several current nodes are very long single-line summaries after the direct manipulation and preview work. This weakens the file's role as a grep-friendly knowledge index and repeats the compaction drift that earlier tickets had cleaned up. Acceptance: bring the node count back within the stated compact target or deliberately revise the target, shorten or split the largest nodes without losing project anchors, and preserve the first five format nodes unchanged.
close_reason: Recompressed MIND_MAP to 50 nodes and folded preview/icon details into existing nodes.

title: Browser smoke only proves direct gesture start, not drop completion
description: Recent smoke probes verify pending trays, but hand-card and CSS fallback helpers only dispatch dragstart and inspect payload, while the Three.js probe drags a piece to a small offset and expects Needs choice rather than a legal target or confirmation. This can pass with broken drop target wiring, target field mapping, or Confirm path in the browser. Acceptance: add at least one browser smoke flow that performs a real source-to-legal-target drop, completes any missing orientation, damage, or card choice through Detailed entry if needed, confirms, and asserts DOM or game-facing state changed; keep existing no-mutation unit tests for rejected paths.
close_reason: Added confirmed CSS fallback direct-drop smoke coverage

title: Automate 3D board smoke verification
description:
Problem: README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md describe important browser smoke checks for the 3D board, but the repository currently has only shared Clojure tests. High-risk behavior such as CDN global loading, WebGL canvas nonblank rendering, texture load failures, orbit/reset controls, mobile sizing, CSS fallback behavior, and raycast selection is still documented as manual or optional automation.

Evidence:
- `clojure -M:test` covers cards, board, pieces, and game-state data only.
- The documented verification checklist includes desktop/mobile canvas and interaction checks that are not encoded in a repeatable command.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add a documented smoke command that can run against a released or dev-served app without making npm part of the default workflow.
- The smoke verifies desktop and mobile viewport widths, a nonblank Three.js canvas, absence of texture/fallback errors in the happy path, visible board content, reset control presence, and selection updating the territory panel.
- Include a fallback-path check for missing WebGL or missing Three globals if the chosen harness can simulate it reliably.
- The command reports actionable failures with enough context to diagnose CDN, asset, extern, or renderer lifecycle regressions.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md name the command and clarify when it should be run.
close_reason: Added clojure -M:smoke browser verification for the 3D board and documented it.

title: Optional hand-trade-only paths are missing for Justice and browser Hanged Man
description: docs/rules.txt says all powers are optional, so multi-power majors should allow fixed-order subsequences rather than forcing the earlier attack or movement step. Pure Hanged Man can already trade hands without Rod when given only a :trade-hand action, but src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc stages Hanged Man at :rod first and never offers a trade-only browser path. Justice has the inverse problem in pure gameplay: apply-sword-move with a Justice hand-trade target but no Sword target still calls the single Sword resolver and fails with :invalid-sword-target, so trade-only Justice is impossible. Acceptance: Justice can resolve a hand-trade-only command, browser Hanged Man can stage/confirm trade-only, and tests cover both optional subsequences while preserving Rod-then-trade and trade-then-Sword flows.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Significantly increase board view brightness
description:
Goal: make the current board view substantially brighter and easier to read.

Requirements:
- Brighten the Three.js board scene so card textures are not dimmed by material colors.
- Increase surrounding board/UI contrast enough that the app no longer reads as very dark overall.
- Preserve the existing tarot image assets, 3D layout, fallback board behavior, and npm-free workflow.
- Keep text and controls readable after the brightness increase.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D card faces render at full or near-full texture brightness.
- The table/canvas and adjacent UI are visibly brighter than the previous dark treatment.
- clojure -M:test passes.
- clojure -M:release succeeds with 0 shadow-cljs compiler warnings.
close_reason: Brightened Three.js board rendering with white card materials, sRGB texture/output encoding, brighter table colors, and a lighter UI palette.

title: Render lying-down pyramids flat against card faces
description: Adjust the Three.js Icehouse pyramid orientation for pieces in a lying-down/cardinal position. A lying pyramid should first be rotated about its long axis by 45 degrees so one side, not an edge, is facing down. It should then receive the small corrective rotation needed for that side plane to be completely parallel to the card face, so the pyramid visually lies flat on the card instead of balancing or clipping awkwardly. Acceptance: lying/cardinal pyramid meshes rest flush on the textured card plane, keep their intended compass direction, do not visibly sink through the card, and still select the underlying territory normally.
close_reason: done

title: hotkey to display more detailed explanation of all special move icons
close_reason: Added G hotkey and special move icon guide

title: Centralize turn-action legality instead of relying on UI source gating
description:
Turn legality is split between UI source availability and individual pure transitions. After a successful move confirmation, the same current player can immediately select another activate/play/draw/orient source; move-source-options remain enabled until the user voluntarily clicks End turn. Direct pure transitions also lack a common guard: apply-draw-move can succeed for a no-piece player, and move facades such as apply-orient-move can mutate a state whose phase is :finished because they mostly only check current-player-id. This undermines the rules.txt turn option model of doing one turn option, with initial placement as the only no-piece option.

Acceptance criteria:
- The game state or app-state records/derives that the current turn action has been consumed, or successful confirmations advance/end the turn according to the intended UX.
- Additional move confirmations are blocked until end-turn or the next turn.
- Public pure move facades consistently reject :finished games, eliminated/non-current players, and no-piece draw attempts with structured errors.
- Tests cover app-state source availability after a successful move and direct pure transition rejection for finished/no-piece states.
close_reason: Centralized public turn-action guards, recorded consumed browser turn actions, blocked repeat confirmations until end-turn, and covered finished/eliminated/non-current/no-piece draw rejections with tests.

title: allow zooming in to go in closer than the current limit
close_reason: Lowered the 3D OrbitControls minimum zoom distance to 3.2 and added smoke coverage for the configured zoom bounds.

title: Disambiguate deck shuffling and deal order in docs
description: MIND_MAP.md node [8] says board/initial-board shuffles before taking nine table cards, while the authoritative create-game path shuffles or orders the full deck once, deals player hands first, then calls board/initial-board with identity on the remaining board deck. Clarify the direct helper behavior versus the create-game setup path so future changes do not double-shuffle or deal the board before hands.
close_reason: Clarified direct board helper shuffle versus create-game deal order in README, AGENTS, and mind map docs.

title: Epic: Complete remaining Gnostica gameplay rules
description:
Track the gameplay rules still missing or only partially wired after the implemented setup, draw/discard, Cup, Rod, Disc, single Sword, orient-piece, and initial-placement flows.

Scope comes from README.md, AGENTS.md, MIND_MAP.md, and docs/rules.txt. The biggest gaps are game lifecycle rules, official bidding, full major-arcana sequencing, browser Sun staging, deferred Sword-major composites, and remaining major arcana powers.
close_reason: All gameplay rules child tickets completed and documentation reconciled

title: Implement scoring, challenge, victory, and elimination
description:
Implement the winning rules from docs/rules.txt: scores are based on territory occupied only by one players pieces, target score defaults to 9 with optional 8 or 10 point games, players may announce a final-turn challenge, and that challenge resolves after their next turn as either a win or elimination.

Current state has score, challenge, and target-score fields, but no full rule transition or browser flow.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Add browser-free scoring helpers: spot territories count 1, royalty 2, major 3, wasteland 0.
- Score only territories occupied exclusively by the scoring players pieces; contested or enemy-only territories do not count.
- Add an end-turn challenge command or transition that records a pending final turn and rejects a second pending challenge.
- Resolve a pending challenge after that players next turn: reaching target score wins, missing it eliminates the player.
- Elimination removes all of that players pieces from board and wastelands and discards their hand while preserving card and stash accounting.
- Support target scores 8, 9, and 10 from setup options or lobby options.
- Cover pure state, schema invariants, app-state confirmation, and feature scenarios.
close_reason: done

title: Implement official bid-to-start setup flow
description:
Implement the official starting-player rule from docs/rules.txt: players secretly bid a card, bids are revealed, highest numbered major wins if any major is bid, otherwise highest minor wins by king through ace order, ties are set aside and rebid, bid cards are redrawn counterclockwise starting with the player to the right of the winner, and the winner goes first.

Current setup records bid metadata but game creation starts directly with deterministic turn order.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Add pure bidding state transitions for selecting bid cards, revealing bids, tie rebids, and finalizing the starting player.
- Major bid comparison follows tarot major numbering; minor comparison follows king, queen, knight, page, ten through ace.
- Tied winning bids are set aside and unavailable until the bid resolves.
- After a winner is selected, players redraw from bid cards in counterclockwise order starting right of the winner until hands return to six or bid cards are exhausted.
- Winner becomes current player and turn order proceeds clockwise from that player.
- Preserve unique card accounting across hands, bid set-aside cards, draw pile, discard pile, and board.
- Add feature scenarios for major win, minor win, tie and rebid, and redraw order.
close_reason: Implemented official browser-free starting bid flow

title: Add major-arcana sequencing engine
description:
Add shared browser-free support for ordered optional multi-power major arcana actions. Rules include hand-play sources making all owned pieces minions, territory activation using pieces touching that card, affected owned pieces becoming minions for later powers in that turn, source card cost charged once, optional powers, fixed power order, and same-target shortcut hooks.

This should provide the reusable foundation for Lovers, Chariot, Justice, Hanged Man, Death, Temperance, Moon, Sun browser staging, and similar major-card flows.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Define a stable command shape for ordered major-arcana action sequences with explicit source, selected actions, minions, targets, and optional shortcut markers.
- Validate that action order matches the card text and cannot be reversed.
- Allow players to omit later optional powers while preserving the rule that selected powers execute in order.
- Charge a hand-source major card exactly once even when multiple sub-actions run.
- For board activation, track eligible minions from pieces on the activated territory plus owned pieces directly affected by earlier sub-actions.
- Preserve existing single-action Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, orient, and placement transition contracts.
- Add pure tests for order rejection, source-cost-once behavior, minion eligibility propagation, and no-mutation-on-failure.
close_reason: Added browser-free major arcana sequencing engine with source resolution, single source cost, ordered optional actions, affected-minion promotion, shortcut hooks, tests, and docs.

title: Wire browser Sun Cup-then-Disc staging
description:
Browser move selection currently exposes Sun as a distinct power but confirms it to :move-transition-unavailable. Pure game-state/apply-sun-move already supports Cup followed by optional Disc plus created-piece and created-territory shortcuts.

Implement staged browser selection and confirmation for the full Sun sequence while keeping separate Cup and Disc selections available for the card.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Move-selection stages Sun as Cup first, then optional Disc, never Disc before Cup.
- Supports Sun from hand-card and board-territory sources with source cost applied once.
- Supports normal Cup then Disc action targeting for pieces and territories.
- Supports same-created-target shortcuts: create a medium piece without spending a small piece, and create a royalty territory in wasteland without first spending a one-point card.
- Rejections preserve staged selection and do not mutate :game.
- Update app-state tests and browser control option tests for Sun commands.
- Remove the Sun-specific :move-transition-unavailable path once staging is complete.
close_reason: Implemented browser Sun Cup-then-Disc staging

title: Implement deferred Sword-major composite rules
description:
Complete the Sword-related major arcana rules that are currently deferred in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md: Justice trades hands before Sword, Death performs Sword twice with same-target shortcut, Tower orients a minion before Sword, and Moon performs Rod then Sword with its full-territory exception.

Single Sword attacks and Tower discard-pile territory replacement already exist; this ticket is for sequencing and special pre/post behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Justice trades hands with the owner of the targeted piece before resolving Sword.
- Death can resolve one or two Sword actions, can split them across minions, and supports same-target shortcut damage without requiring intermediate replacement resources.
- Tower stages and applies minion orientation before validating the Sword target while preserving existing discard-pile replacement behavior.
- Moon stages Rod then Sword in order and allows entering a full territory only when the Sword step leaves no more than three pieces there.
- Hand-source cards are discarded exactly once for each composite major move.
- Board-source activation respects eligible minions for each sub-action.
- Add unit, app-state, and feature coverage for success and rejection cases.
close_reason: done

title: Implement draw, redraw, and discard-pile major powers
description:
Implement major arcana powers centered on deck, hand, and discard-pile manipulation: Fool reveals and optionally plays the next draw-pile card twice, High Priestess performs two discard-and-redraw passes, and Judgement draws selected cards from anywhere in the discard pile up to the minion pip count and hand limit.

Standalone draw/discard-to-six already exists, but these card powers have distinct source, minion, discard, and selection rules.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Fool reveals the top draw-pile card, allows its power to be used or skipped, then repeats once.
- Fool preserves card accounting for revealed cards, hand-source Fool discard, and any generated sub-action source costs.
- High Priestess supports two sequential redraw passes, each discarding any or no hand cards and drawing up to six or fewer.
- Judgement lets the active minion choose cards from anywhere in discard pile up to that minions pip count and the six-card hand limit.
- Hand-source Judgement can draw itself back from discard when legal.
- All paths support deterministic discard-pile reshuffle behavior where relevant.
- Add feature scenarios for optional use, hand limit rejections, and card accounting.
close_reason: Implemented pure Fool, High Priestess, and Judgement draw/discard major powers with tests and docs.

title: Implement Hierophant, Hermit, and Devil powers
description:
Implement the remaining non-suit piece and territory manipulation majors: Hierophant replaces the target piece with one of the current players pieces of the same size and orients it, Hermit moves a targeted piece or territory to an eligible empty space, and Devil orients targeted pieces three times including enemy pieces.

These powers do not map cleanly to the current Cup/Rod/Disc/Sword single-action transition set and need explicit pure transitions plus browser staging.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Hierophant validates target-piece reach, requires same-size current-player stash availability, replaces the target piece, returns the replaced piece to its owner stash, and orients the new piece.
- Hermit can move a targeted piece to any empty territory or wasteland, preserving enemy orientation and allowing own-piece orientation.
- Hermit can move a targeted territory to an eligible wasteland not occupied by enemy pieces, preserving pieces and orientations according to the rule text.
- Devil can orient a target piece three times, including enemy pieces, and recalculates legal targets after orienting the acting minion.
- All transitions preserve stash mirrors, card accounting, board coordinates, and schema invariants.
- Add browser staging and feature scenarios for each power.
close_reason: done

title: Implement remaining composite suit-major powers
description:
Implement the major arcana that compose existing suit actions but need ordered multi-action behavior: Empress orient then unbounded Cup, Emperor orient then unbounded Rod, Lovers Rod then Cup, Chariot Rod twice with same-piece pass-through shortcut, Hanged Man Rod then hand trade, and Temperance Cup twice.

Some individual variants already exist, such as Empress-style Cup and Emperor-style Rod, but their full ordered card text is not yet staged or applied as a single major-card move.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Empress stages orient-minion before Cup and keeps the existing unbounded Cup target behavior.
- Emperor stages orient-minion before Rod and keeps the existing unbounded Rod destination behavior.
- Lovers stages Rod then Cup in order, including minion eligibility after Rod movement or push effects.
- Chariot supports one or two Rod actions and same-piece pass-through through void or full territories while forbidding ending there.
- Hanged Man stages Rod then trades hands with the owner of the targeted piece.
- Temperance supports one or two Cup actions, including split minions and source cost once.
- Add tests proving action order cannot be reversed and optional later powers may be skipped.
close_reason: Implemented composite suit major powers

title: Implement World major-arcana delegation
description:
Implement World: it has the power of any major arcana territory currently on the board. This requires enumerating eligible major territories, choosing one copied power, and applying that power with World as the source while respecting the copied card text.

This should integrate with the major-arcana sequencing engine rather than duplicating every major power implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- World move selection lists current major arcana territories on the board as copy targets.
- Copied powers use World as the source for hand discard/source-cost purposes but follow the selected territory cards major-arcana power text.
- World cannot copy absent major cards, minor territories, wastelands, or destroyed territories.
- Works for board-source and hand-source World commands where legal.
- Reuses the shared major sequencing engine for ordered multi-power cards.
- Add pure and app-state tests for copying at least one single-power major and one composite major.
close_reason: done

title: Game schema allows duplicate active piece ids
description: The backend game-state schema accepts duplicate :id values under [:pieces :on-board]. Piece ids are used as stable identifiers by transition helpers, and functions such as replace-piece-by-id and remove-piece-by-id operate by id, so duplicate active ids can cause multi-piece mutation/removal and stash corruption if introduced by fixtures, tests, imports, or malformed state construction. Repro: create-game, seed two active pieces with the same :id using gnostica.game-state/with-board-pieces, then gnostica.game-schema/valid-game? returns true and explain-game returns nil.
